Â Â Rasmus Andresen, im Namen der Verts/ALE-Fraktion. â Herr PrÃ¤sident! Stellen Sie sich vor, wir wachen in zwei Wochen auf und Donald Trump ist wieder US-PrÃ¤sident. Wer von uns glaubt ernsthaft, dass die EuropÃ¤ische Union auf so ein Szenario vorbereitet ist? Und als Haushaltspolitiker kann ich sagen: Haushaltspolitisch sind wir es auf jeden Fall nicht. Denn anstatt mehr zu investieren, was wir dringend mÃ¼ssten, haben uns die Staats- und Regierungschefs in der Vergangenheit sogar KÃ¼rzungen eingebrocktÂ â KÃ¼rzungen bei Forschung, KÃ¼rzungen bei Gesundheit, KÃ¼rzungen bei Zukunftsthemen. Gerade von den Staats- und Regierungschefs wie Olaf Scholz oder Emmanuel Macron mÃ¼ssen wir hier deutlich mehr erwarten.

Denn wir brauchen einen Haushalt, der so aufgestellt ist, dass wir auÃenpolitisch souverÃ¤n und wirtschaftlich wettbewerbsfÃ¤hig werden. Und wir brauchen einen Haushalt, der allen Menschen in der EuropÃ¤ischen Union eine Zukunftsperspektive gibt, inklusive sozialer Absicherung. Stattdessen lassen wir unsere Infrastruktur verrotten, und wir sorgen dafÃ¼r, dass immer mehr Menschen in der EuropÃ¤ischen Union in Armut abrutschenÂ â das ist nicht zukunftsgerecht.

Und nein, wir kÃ¶nnen hier als Parlament mit dem Haushalt 2025 nicht jedes Problem lÃ¶sen, aber als Parlament formulieren wir kleine Schritte in die richtige Richtung, und jetzt kommt es darauf an, dafÃ¼r zu kÃ¤mpfenÂ â beispielsweise unsere Infrastruktur zu verbessern, indem wir die FazilitÃ¤t âConnecting Europeâ stÃ¤rken, indem wir mehr in Erasmus investieren und dafÃ¼r sorgen, dass mehr junge Menschen an Erasmus teilnehmen kÃ¶nnen, oder auch, indem wir mehr in Gesundheit, vor allem mentale Gesundheit, investieren.

Es wird in den nÃ¤chsten Wochen darauf ankommen, auch bei diesem Haushalt, darauf zu achten, dass wir in die richtige Richtung gehen. Und ich fordere den Rat auf, nicht das zu machen, was Sie immer machenÂ â immer nur KÃ¼rzungen vorschlagen. Diskutieren Sie mit uns Ã¼ber zukunftsfÃ¤hige Haushaltspolitik und seien Sie bereit, auch dafÃ¼r die richtigen Konsequenzen zu ziehen!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â JoÃ£o Oliveira, em nome do Grupo The Left. â Senhor Presidente, o Parlamento Europeu pretende reverter os cortes feitos pelo Conselho Europeu no OrÃ§amento da UniÃ£o Europeia, mas nÃ£o reverte as opÃ§Ãµes erradas que estÃ£o na base deste orÃ§amento. Este orÃ§amento reflete opÃ§Ãµes militaristas e armamentistas e vai ao encontro dos interesses dos grupos econÃ³micos e dos desejos do complexo industrial militar, mas ignora os problemas dos trabalhadores e dos povos.

Esses problemas podem ter ainda alguma consideraÃ§Ã£o a partir das propostas que apresentÃ¡mos. Propomos que as verbas associadas Ã s opÃ§Ãµes militaristas e armamentistas sejam canalizadas para os fundos destinados a dar resposta a problemas sociais. Para que os direitos sociais dos povos sejam garantidos, em vez de serem transformados em negÃ³cios, propomos que o orÃ§amento da UE dÃª apoio aos EstadosâMembros que decidam reverter os processos de privatizaÃ§Ã£o de empresas em setores sociais e estratÃ©gicos.

Propomos a criaÃ§Ã£o de financiamento adicional e especÃ­fico da UniÃ£o Europeia que os EstadosâMembros possam mobilizar para a expansÃ£o e requalificaÃ§Ã£o dos parques habitacionais pÃºblicos e a defesa do direito Ã  habitaÃ§Ã£o digna e a preÃ§os acessÃ­veis. Propomos que os fundos europeus estejam condicionados Ã  proteÃ§Ã£o do emprego, ao respeito pelos direitos dos trabalhadores e ao contributo para o desenvolvimento local e regional.Â Propomos o reforÃ§o de verbas do Fundo Social Europeu+, uma dotaÃ§Ã£o especÃ­fica para a Garantia para a InfÃ¢ncia e o apoio a redes pÃºblicas de cuidados a crianÃ§as, idosos e pessoas com deficiÃªncia.

Propomos o aumento de verbas para as fileiras produtivas, nomeadamente nas pescas, no vinho, no azeite ou na apicultura. Propomos ainda a criaÃ§Ã£o de um programa POSEI Transportes que dÃª resposta Ã s necessidades especÃ­ficas das populaÃ§Ãµes das regiÃµes ultraperifÃ©ricas, como os AÃ§ores e a Madeira.

Na votaÃ§Ã£o destas propostas, haverÃ¡ ainda uma oportunidade para colocar os direitos e interesses dos povos como prioridade.

Â Â Isabel Benjumea Benjumea (PPE). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±or comisario, seÃ±or ministro, afrontar un presupuesto es una grandÃ­sima responsabilidad porque tenemos que decidir a quÃ© gastos y a quÃ© inversiones vamos a destinar el dinero proveniente de los impuestos de los ciudadanos.

Por lo tanto, hay que hacer tres cosas cuando afrontamos un nuevo presupuesto. En primer lugar, evaluar los programas que sÃ­ funcionan, aquellos que estÃ¡n teniendo el impacto deseado, que estÃ¡n generando las oportunidades que queremos para los europeos y que, ademÃ¡s, estÃ¡n cubriendo esas necesidades bÃ¡sicas que queremos cubrir desde la UniÃ³n Europea. TambiÃ©n tenemos que determinar esas necesidades a las que hay que destinar mÃ¡s recursos porque se ha dado esa situaciÃ³n, como puede ser, en este caso en el presupuesto, el caso de Frontex. Y en Ãºltimo lugar hay que determinar nuevas demandas, como puede ser ây es importante hablar de elloâ el pago de los intereses de la deuda de los Next Generation EU.

Esto es lo que hace este presupuesto. Por un lado, mantiene y refuerza lo que funciona: Erasmus+, la polÃ­tica agrÃ­cola comÃºn, la polÃ­tica de cohesiÃ³n, las inversiones en innovaciÃ³n. Y lo mantiene porque funciona. Por otro lado, refuerza algo que estamos demandando, que es reforzar nuestras fronteras, a saber, Frontex. Y, por otro lado, da una soluciÃ³n, que no es la ideal, sobre cÃ³mo hacer frente a los intereses que tenemos que pagar de la deuda de los fondos Next Generation EU. Y digo que no es ideal porque nos tiene que hacer reflexionar de cara a nuevos debates que estÃ¡ habiendo sobre crear nuevos fondos tambiÃ©n con cargo a la deuda.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Nils UÅ¡akovs (S&D). â Mr President, dear colleagues, I will use this opportunity to talk about European administration. The recent incident at Strasbourg train station after the last plenary â which left thousands of employees stranded â has once again highlighted the inefficiency and, at the same time, the financial burden of the so-called 'travelling circus' that the European institutions endure every month. The annual cost is EURÂ 180Â million, and it really raises questions about the cost-effectiveness of this event.

We also have a historical practice of maintaining multiple European institutions in different Member States, such as, for example, Luxembourg â probably the most beautiful, yet also the most expensive Member State, especially with respect to housing. We pay salaries that 90Â % of Latvians wouldn't even dream of owning and, at the same time, for certain employees the salaries are lower than the Luxembourg minimum wage. As a result, the European institutions struggle to hire the talent they need to fulfil their primary functions.

Every year, the demands on the European institutions grow as we assign new responsibilities. Yet the financial resources provided do not match these increasing demands. For example, the European Union External Action Service, which operates globally, is forced to maintain offices in substandard, unsecure conditions, sometimes having to rent additional premises in order to provide and to prevent the risk of ceilings collapsing on the heads of staff.

Other institutions have to request budget transfers as early as the summer, because the initial allocations are insufficient to cover basic operational costs, such as heating or electricity bills. I am talking right now not about the budget alone, but also about the new MMF, because we are spending hundreds of millions of euros in administration and, at the same time, we face the situation we just discussed with the External Action Service.

We are discussing the usage of special instruments, such as the single market instrument to finance the needs of the administration. We are discussing the probability of taking funds from essential European programmes. That means that the European Parliament and the Council have to take the challenge and to start collaborating in order to provide a new MMF that will be dealing with hundreds of millions of taxpayers' money in the most cost-effective way.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Julien Sanchez (PfE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, mes chers collÃ¨gues, en tant que nouveau membre de cette assemblÃ©e, je suis dâabord frappÃ© de dÃ©couvrir, Ã  chaque rapport de la Cour europÃ©enne des comptes, la mauvaise gestion des fonds de lâUnion europÃ©enne, avec des taux dâerreur indÃ©cents rÃ©guliÃ¨rement constatÃ©s dans presque toutes les rubriques budgÃ©taires et des dÃ©faillances persistantes dans les systÃ¨mes de gestion et de contrÃ´le, ce qui constitue un signal dâalarme quant Ã  lâefficacitÃ© des dÃ©penses europÃ©ennes.

Jâai Ã©tÃ© maire dix ans, et avant dâÃªtre Ã©lu ici je pensais que ces institutions, malgrÃ© leur fonctionnement dogmatique et sectaire, Ã©taient au moins des institutions sÃ©rieuses. Je constate quâil nâen est rien et que le budget de lâUnion europÃ©enne est beaucoup plus mal gÃ©rÃ© que le budget dâune collectivitÃ© locale en France.

Nous sommes ici face Ã  un budget de 191Â milliardsÂ dâeuros, qui dissimule un double dÃ©ficit rÃ©el. Dâune part, il y a un dÃ©ficit par dÃ©budgÃ©tisation. En effet, les articlesÂ 6, 7 etÂ 8 du rÃ¨glement financier de lâUnion exigent que des milliards de crÃ©dits de diverses masses financiÃ¨res, aujourdâhui classÃ©s sous les termes dâinstruments, mÃ©canismes ou facilitÃ©s, soient rÃ©intÃ©grÃ©s dans les lignes budgÃ©taires. Or ces fonds sont soustraits de tout contrÃ´le dÃ©mocratique depuis des annÃ©es. Ce budget est donc insincÃ¨re.

Dâautre part, il y a un dÃ©ficit par dissimulation, notamment pour compenser la hausse des taux dâintÃ©rÃªt du programme NextGenerationEU, passÃ©s de moins de 1Â % Ã  plus de 3Â %. PlutÃ´t que dâaffronter cette rÃ©alitÃ©, la Commission et le Conseil jouent sur les marges budgÃ©taires et sur lâinstrument de flexibilitÃ©, crÃ©ant ainsi un artifice comptable peu durable.

La raison de ce chaos budgÃ©taire? Une ambition politique dÃ©mesurÃ©e dâune part et une vision idÃ©ologique Ã©troite dâautre part. Dâun cÃ´tÃ©, nous voyons une Commission europÃ©enne assoiffÃ©e dâÃ©largissement Ã  lâEst, dont les pays ne seraient, en plus, pas des contributeurs nets au budget de lâUnion europÃ©enne, et dâinterventions coÃ»teuses, particuliÃ¨rement en Ukraine, oÃ¹ la paix nâest plus un objectif de lâUnion europÃ©enne, qui ne parle que de verser de lâargent encore et toujours, mais sans Ã©voquer un quelconque objectif dâune issue diplomatique. De lâautre, une idÃ©ologie malthusienne qui a entravÃ© les investissements essentiels dans nos infrastructures de santÃ© et notre industrie.

Quand un budget est mal gÃ©rÃ©, il y a deux solutions: stop ou encore. Vous ne voulez pas tailler dans les mauvaises dÃ©penses, car cela vous obligerait Ã  retrousser les manches. Vous voulez toujours plus de recettes, vous imaginez mÃªme de nouvelles taxes, mais les dispositifs imaginÃ©s, au-delÃ  dâÃªtre complexes et inefficaces, sont dÃ©risoires, illusoires ou Ã  tout le moins provisoires.

Nous nous y opposons fermement et refuserons tout impÃ´t europÃ©en qui viendrait alourdir encore la charge fiscale des citoyens. Il est absurde de vouloir compter sur ces mÃ©canismes pour financer lâavenir de lâUnion. Nous avons dÃ©posÃ© de nombreux amendements et nous espÃ©rons quâil se trouvera une majoritÃ© de gens courageux et sÃ©rieux pour les voter, sans quoi nous voterons bien sÃ»r contre ce budget.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ruggero Razza (ECR). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Ministro, signor Commissario, ho sentito alcuni toni un po' entusiasti nel descrivere sia la proposta proveniente dalla Commissione sia il compromesso raggiunto nel lavoro di commissione.

E io non voglio pensare, secondo la retorica ascoltata, che questo sia il primo bilancio della nuova legislatura, perchÃ© una legislatura che si Ã¨ aperta con una richiesta di maggiore competitivitÃ  e di investimenti in Europa non li trova nel suo primo bilancio. Una legislatura che si Ã¨ aperta con una grande attesa da parte dei cittadini su alcuni temi essenziali â penso a quello della sanitÃ  â non lo trova nel bilancio.

Certo, si partiva da un taglio di miliardo di euro sul quadro pluriennale e si arriva comunque a un taglio della sanitÃ , che fa il contraltare ad un ulteriore taglio, che Ã¨ quello della capacitÃ  del nostro sistema di far crescere l'economia.

E allora io voglio pensare, signor Ministro, che si potranno raggiungere i giusti compromessi ma voglio soprattutto pensare che questo non sia il primo bilancio della nuova legislatura, ma che sia l'ultimo della vecchia.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Kai Tegethoff (Verts/ALE). â MrÂ President, I'm glad there is a compromise. And we support this compromise. Nevertheless, I am disappointed in this House's unwillingness to take the steps that are really needed to tackle the climate crisis. For example, the political groups could not even agree on adequate funding for the European Railway Agency.

As Enrico Letta pointed out in his report, there is a growing risk that the 2030 targets for rail infrastructure will not be met. The modal shift to rail is our joint declared goal, and we are supposed to spend 30Â % of the EU budget on climate-related actions, but this is not being reflected in the budget that we are talking about today.

As the European Parliament, we should take a clear stand on this. We should fund the European Railway Agency in a way that reflects the critical need to make modal shift a reality and to improve rail safety. We should demand the Member States start acting and start investing in climate-friendly transport infrastructure and have the core part of the European railway network ready by 2030.

We have tabled two further amendments to improve these factors, at least in the surroundings of the European Parliament. Since we can't stop this insane practice of travelling almost 500Â km for every plenary session, we want to make it at least a little bit more sustainable and increase Parliament's charter train capacity.

And second, we should not only install solar panels on the roofs of our parliamentary buildings, but also clearly commit to phasing out fossil fuel heating and cooling systems. I really hope for your support. Let's lead by example.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Pasquale Tridico (The Left). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, Torino, Melfi, Termoli, Pomigliano, ma anche Bruxelles, Zwickau: le fabbriche di auto chiuse o in crisi sono dappertutto in Europa.

Solo in Italia sono 100.000 i posti di lavoro a rischio nel settore dell'automotive e l'indotto fatto da piccole e medie imprese Ã¨ il piÃ¹ penalizzato. Se non vogliamo far sparire questa industria europea, dobbiamo intervenire subito, come durante il COVID, durante il quale grazie al programma SURE solo in Italia abbiamo salvato quasi 500.000 posti di lavoro.

La crisi dell'auto Ã¨ europea: serve dunque una risposta europea. Abbiamo presentato un emendamento al bilancio dell'Unione del 2025, in cui proponiamo l'istituzione di un nuovo fondo SURE valido per due anni per l'automotive per 100 miliardi di euro.

In passato abbiamo distribuito soldi a pioggia e i grandi manager dell'auto hanno continuato a distribuire dividendi mentre i lavoratori erano in cassa integrazione. Con questo nuovo SURE voltiamo pagina: salviamo i posti di lavoro, aiutiamo l'industria ma obblighiamo le case automobilistiche a fare maggiori investimenti per la transizione verso l'elettrico, per la formazione e per non licenziare lavoratori.

Il bilancio europeo all'1,1% Ã¨ troppo piccolo: non ha nessuna ambizione e rispetto alle sfide globali continua ad avere una dimensione insignificante. Adesso abbiamo una grande occasione: una causa comune in Europa sulla quale investire per dare maggiore rilevanza all'Unione. SURE per l'automotive Ã¨ l'occasione.

Mi appello dunque a tutti gli europarlamentari: salviamo l'industria europea dell'auto, salviamo centinaia di posti di lavoro, sostenete l'istituzione di un nuovo SURE per l'automotive.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Thomas Geisel (NI). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Herr Minister, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Ich bin ja noch nicht lange in diesem Parlament, aber ich habe den Eindruck, beim Haushalt gilt eigentlich immer dasselbe Verfahren: Die Kommission legt einen Haushaltsentwurf vor, der Rat streicht ihn anschlieÃend etwas zusammen, und dann kommt das Parlament, stellt den Entwurf der Kommission wieder her und legt hier und da noch eine Schippe drauf. Ich bin nicht sicher, ob wir mit diesem Ritual unserer Verantwortung und unserer Aufgabe als Parlamentarier wirklich gerecht werden.

Es ist bedauerlich genug, dass dieses Parlament nicht das Recht hat, selbst Gesetzesinitiativen einzubringen, und eigentlich nur darÃ¼ber entscheiden kann, was ihm von der Kommission vorgelegt wird. Umso wichtiger ist es, dass das Parlament seine Aufgabe wahrnimmt, die Kommission zu beaufsichtigen und zu kontrollieren. Und dabei kann es nicht darum gehen, der Kommission immer neue Kompetenzen zu geben und immer weitere Mittel zur VerfÃ¼gung zu stellen. Es gilt vielmehr das SubsidiaritÃ¤tsprinzip: Die Kommission sollte nur die Aufgaben Ã¼bernehmen, mit denen die Mitgliedstaaten Ã¼berfordert sind, Aufgaben, die eben nicht bÃ¼rgernÃ¤her, transparenter und effizienter von den Mitgliedstaaten, vielleicht auch ihren Provinzen und ihren Kommunen Ã¼bernommen werden kÃ¶nnten. Und wir sollten sorgsam darauf achten, dass mit dem Steuergeld der EuropÃ¤er sorgsam und sparsam umgegangen wird.

Ich habe nicht den Eindruck, dass diese Gesichtspunkte hinreichend berÃ¼cksichtigt wurden bei diesem Haushalt.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â DanuÅ¡e NerudovÃ¡ (PPE). â MrÂ President, dear colleagues, President von der Leyen stood here in this house of democracy and declared that this mandate should focus on the largest investment in our competitiveness ever.

Instead, we are witnessing short-sighted cuts in strategic budget lines from the Member States. A cut in Horizon Europe, our flagship programme from innovation and research: experts are telling us we should double its funding, yet we are slashing it. This isn't a subsidy or expense. It's an investment to our future. A cut in Erasmus, the most successful policy the EU has ever implemented. How do we expect to boost mobility and skills-sharing across our continent if we reduce its funding? A cut in our health programmes at a time when the US spends USDÂ 46Â billion annually on health research. We invest only a quarter of that.

Dear colleagues, some states want to keep building our economy on subsidies instead of boosting investments with real European value. Fortunately, we in Parliament have the responsibility and power to push back on these propositions. I am confident that together we can succeed in protecting these crucial policies for the future of the Union.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Giuseppe Lupo (S&D). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, Commissione, condivido la posizione espressa dal nostro relatore, l'onorevole Negrescu, e non condivido per niente la posizione del Consiglio di disconoscere gli accordi sul pagamento degli interessi del Next Generation EU e di tagliare i programmi Orizzonte, Erasmus e altri per circa un miliardo e mezzo di euro dal bilancio 2025, indebolendo cosÃ¬ l'economia e la competitivitÃ  dell'Unione europea e degli Stati membri.

Presidente, Commissione, colleghi, la crescita e la competitivitÃ  dell'Unione europea e degli Stati membri si rilanciano rafforzando gli investimenti e respingendo i tagli.

Â Â Olivier Chastel (Renew). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, chers collÃ¨gues, un budget, câest Ã©videmment davantage que des colonnes de chiffres. Câest la traduction de nos prioritÃ©s politiques. Et nos prioritÃ©s politiques pour lâexerciceÂ 2025, tout comme pour le prochain cadre financier pluriannuel dâailleurs, doivent Ãªtre orientÃ©es vers les investissements destinÃ©s Ã  dÃ©velopper notre autonomie stratÃ©gique. Â Â Rasmus Nordqvist (Verts/ALE). â Mr President, dear Commissioners, dear Council, any budget discussion is actually a discussion about whether we are trying to adjust reality to the budget in front of us, or should we try and adjust the budget to this reality we are facing?

I have an example. For as long as I've been following EU budget negotiations â first in the national parliament and now here in the European Parliament â it's a clear case when it comes to humanitarian aid: we are looking at an attempt to actually adjust the reality to the budget. In the short time I've been here, we already amended the budgets for 2024 numerous times, adding more money to the field.

We have several armed conflicts in the world. We see climate crises attacking the poorest people in the world, and still we don't see a humanitarian budget actually answering these questions. So my recommendation for the future is clear: we must be better at adjusting the budget to the reality, and not the other way around.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Rudi Kennes (The Left). â Voorzitter, collegaâs, de werknemers in Europa krijgen hun facturen niet meer betaald. Maar hier worden gewoon lonen uitbetaald tot veertienduizend euro. De voorzitter en haar Europese Commissie ontvangen zelfs salarissen tussen de twintig- Ã  dertigduizend euro per maand. Als het kassaticket van de supermarkt ontploft en energie- of brandstofprijzen de hoogte inschieten, is dat voor de gewone mens een nachtmerrie. Hier daarentegen voelt men daar weinig of niks van en daarom wordt er ook niets aan gedaan.

Erger nog, vanuit de ivoren Commissietoren komt men ons vandaag vertellen dat we allemaal onze broekriem een beetje moeten aantrekken. Dat er geen geld meer is voor pensioenen, scholen, onderwijs, ziekenhuizen of ander sociaal beleid.

Daarom is en blijft ons jaarlijks voorstel van de PVDA even klaar als duidelijk. Doe iets aan die privileges en verlaag deze duizelingwekkende salarissen. Zo krijgt men hier tenminste een idee van de impact van de beslissingen die hier genomen worden. We hebben nieuwe normen en waarden nodig voor onze politieke leiders in Europa. Wij zijn hier allemaal om het volk te dienen, niet onszelf.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Monika Hohlmeier (PPE). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Herr Kommissar, liebe RatsprÃ¤sidentschaft, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Der europÃ¤ische Haushalt ist so vielfÃ¤ltig und facettenreich wie kaum ein anderer Haushalt. Im kommenden Jahr 2025 gibt es so viele dringende Themen, dass wir uns kaum um alle kÃ¼mmern kÃ¶nnen. Aber ich mÃ¶chte vor allem drei Bereiche angehen.

Erstens: Horizon Europe, wie vorhin schon von der Kollegin erwÃ¤hnt. Ich mÃ¶chte es nicht nur von der Seite der KÃ¼rzung her angehenÂ â und dass der Rat da immer noch mehr kÃ¼rzen mÃ¶chte, ist eher peinlich, weil nÃ¤mlich die unglaubliche Anzahl von 70Â % der qualitativ hochwertigen VorschlÃ¤ge nicht finanziert werden kÃ¶nnen. Und da reden wir dann gleichzeitig von WettbewerbsfÃ¤higkeit!

Zweitens: das Thema Grenzschutz. Der Rat redet viel vom AuÃengrenzschutz, aber wir mÃ¼ssen ihn endlich vollstÃ¤ndig realisieren, und wir mÃ¼ssen den Mitgliedstaaten, die besonders unter Druck stehen, bei InfrastrukturmaÃnahmen wie auch bei sonstigen MaÃnahmen und auch personell helfen, damit sie die Aufgaben lÃ¶sen kÃ¶nnen, die im Migrationspakt auch vom Rat mit beschlossen worden sind.

Und drittens: HÃ¤ufig wird behauptet, dass UNRWA direkt, sozusagen die EuropÃ¤ische Union finanziert Terrorismus. Nein, wir finanzieren keinen Terrorismus, aber es werden Gelder entwendet, und es werden Gelder der UN missbraucht. Und wir mÃ¼ssen auch dagegen vorgehen, dass es LÃ¤nder gibt, die Terrorismus direkt finanzieren, und das bedeutet in der Konsequenz: Unsere Hilfe muss der ZivilbevÃ¶lkerung gelten, und das in vielfÃ¤ltiger Form, und deshalb sollten wir die Diskussion nicht auf UNRWA konzentrieren, sondern wir sollten schauen, dass wir jede Form der Hilfe fÃ¼r die ZivilbevÃ¶lkerung und vor allem fÃ¼r Kinder, Frauen, Alte und Kranke realisieren kÃ¶nnen.

PRESIDENZA: ANTONELLA SBERNA

Â Â Carla Tavares (S&D). â Senhora Presidente, Caros Colegas, a resoluÃ§Ã£o que hoje discutimos em plenÃ¡rio representa o esforÃ§o dos relatores para alcanÃ§ar um sÃ³lido pacote de compromissos para o orÃ§amento de 2025. Ã a resoluÃ§Ã£o que melhor serve os cidadÃ£os, a sociedade europeia, as empresas, os jovens. ReforÃ§a os programas de apoio Ã s pessoas no terreno e nas zonas remotas, bem como os programas de apoio ao diÃ¡logo social, que permitem uma maior coordenaÃ§Ã£o dos sistemas de seguranÃ§a social dos EstadosâMembros â Ã¡reas de vital importÃ¢ncia para uma Europa mais coesa e competitiva.

ComissÃ¡rio, apesar do acordo alcanÃ§ado na revisÃ£o intercalar do QFP sobre a cobertura dos custos de derrapagem dos juros do NextGenerationEU, esta nÃ£o Ã© uma soluÃ§Ã£o que possa subsistir para alÃ©m deste QFP. Precisamos de uma outra soluÃ§Ã£o a partir de 2028. Claramente, o Conselho nÃ£o se mostra inclinado a cumprir seja o acordado nesta sede, seja o acordado em matÃ©ria de recursos prÃ³prios cruciais para, a partir de 2028, reembolsarmos a dÃ­vida.

Â Â AntÃ³nio TÃ¢nger CorrÃªa (PfE). â Senhora Presidente, Senhor ComissÃ¡rio, Caros Colegas, o orÃ§amento da UniÃ£o Europeia para 2025 deve dar prioridade aos nossos cidadÃ£os na eficiÃªncia, na seguranÃ§a e na proteÃ§Ã£o dos nossos valores, cortar gastos desnecessÃ¡rios e simplificar processos administrativos, em vez de financiar uma mÃ¡quina burocrÃ¡tica, pesada, inflexÃ­vel e distante dos cidadÃ£os; nomeadamente, o caso da SuÃ­Ã§a, a aÃ§Ã£o externa da UniÃ£o Europeia.

Â Â Joachim Streit (Renew). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, wertes PrÃ¤sidium, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Der jÃ¼ngste Bericht des EuropÃ¤ischen Rechnungshofes zeigt klar: Der EU-Haushalt muss effizienter gestaltet werden. Die Fehlerquote bei den Ausgaben liegt bei 5,6Â %, und in den kritischen Ausgabenbereichen erreichen wir sogar 8Â % Fehlerquote. Diese alarmierenden Zahlen haben wir seit der Finanzkrise nicht mehr gesehen. Die KomplexitÃ¤t und Ãberschneidung vieler Programme fÃ¼hren zu Fehlern und unnÃ¶tiger Verschwendung. Das zeigt uns: Mehr Geld ist nicht die LÃ¶sung.

Der EU-Haushalt muss sich daher wieder auf die Kernthemen konzentrieren, die den Menschen Sorgen bereiten: Investitionen in eine starke Wirtschaft, eine geregelte Migrationspolitik, Sicherheit und bezahlbare Energie. Statt nach neuen Einnahmequellen zu suchen, sollten wir bestehende Programme verbessern, BÃ¼rokratie abbauen. So erreichen wir unsere politischen Ziele und sichern die finanzielle StabilitÃ¤t der EU, ohne die BÃ¼rger zusÃ¤tzlich zu belasten.

Â Â Estrella GalÃ¡n (The Left). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, cada dÃ­a, treceÂ personas pierden la vida en el mar intentando llegar a Europa para poner su vida a salvo; entretanto, este Parlamento quiere aprobar milÂ millones deÂ euros para Frontex, una agencia que cada aÃ±o multiplica su presupuesto mÃ¡s y mÃ¡s, mientras ha sido cuestionada tanto por la violaciÃ³n de derechos humanos como por la gestiÃ³n de sus fondos: milÂ millones deÂ euros destinados a mÃ¡s tragedia, a mÃ¡s dolor y a mÃ¡s muerte. Â¿CuÃ¡ndo se va a priorizar salvar la vida de las personas frente al blindaje de las fronteras?

SeÃ±orÃ­as, estamos a tiempo: hoy traigo aquÃ­ una enmienda en nombre de la sociedad civil, proponiendo que, de esa enorme partida para blindar Europa, para blindar fronteras, 120Â millones deÂ eurosÂ sean destinados a poner en marcha un servicio de rescate y salvamento para que los Estados miembros puedan cumplir con sus obligaciones de Derecho internacional.

Es necesario un cambio de rumbo humanizando las polÃ­ticas migratorias europeas, priorizando la vida de las personas y la protecciÃ³n de la gente: nuestro modelo es el que defienden las organizaciones comprometidas con los derechos humanos, como Open Arms, o la eurodiputada Carola Rackete, la capitana valiente, nuestra compaÃ±era, que puso a salvo a cientos de personas en busca de un puerto seguro.

Â Â HÃ©lder Sousa Silva (PPE). â Senhora Presidente, Caros Colegas, no momento desafiante em que vivemos de transformaÃ§Ã£o global, o OrÃ§amento da UniÃ£o permanece um instrumento fundamental para dar resposta Ã s necessidades e tambÃ©m Ã s aspiraÃ§Ãµes dos nossos concidadÃ£os. Em suma, acredito firmemente que esta proposta de orÃ§amento para 2025 Ã© uma proposta capaz de tornar uma Europa mais forte, capaz de agir e de defender os seus e os nossos interesses e valores democrÃ¡ticos.

Â Â Moritz KÃ¶rner (Renew). â MadamÂ President, dear colleagues, Palestinian schoolbooks teach hate. In science and math books, terrorists are praised. A female terrorist who killed several Jews is cited as an example for female empowerment. Most maps replace Israel with Palestine. Despite our financial support, these books haven't changed for years. But whenever we propose withholding funds until anti-Semitic pages are removed, the Budget Committee rejects our amendment. Please tell me, how can a parliament that always claims to defend fundamental values suddenly oppose removing anti-Semitic content from a schoolbook? What motivates this double standard? The hateful pages could be ripped out within hours and the funds could be released immediately.

Colleagues, this is not about being pro-Israel or being pro-Palestinian. This is about breaking the cycle of hate and ending the teaching of hate. And since we are all paying for it, we can make it happen. The final vote is tomorrow. So let's make this happen.

Î¤Î¿ Î¶Î·ÏÎ¿ÏÎ¼ÎµÎ½Î¿ ÏÎ¿Î»Î»ÏÎ½ ÏÏÏÏÎ½ ÏÎ®Î¼ÎµÏÎ± ÎµÎ¯Î½Î±Î¹ Î½Î± ÏÏÎ¬ÏÎ¾ÎµÎ¹ ÎµÎ»Î¬ÏÏÏÎ½ÏÎ· ÏÏÎ¹Ï Î¿Î¹ÎºÎ¿Î½Î¿Î¼Î¯ÎµÏ Î¼Îµ Î±Î½ÎµÏÏÏÎµÏÎ· ÎºÎ±Î¹ ÎºÎ±Î»ÏÏÎµÏÎ· Î´Î¹Î±ÏÎµÎ¯ÏÎ¹ÏÎ· ÏÏÎ½ Î´Î¹Î±ÏÎºÏÏ Î±ÏÎ¾Î·Î¼Î­Î½ÏÎ½ Î±Î½Î±Î³ÎºÏÎ½ Î³Î¹Î± Î±Î¼ÏÎ½ÏÎ¹ÎºÎ­Ï Î´Î±ÏÎ¬Î½ÎµÏ, Î´Î·Î»Î±Î´Î® Î½Î± Î¼Î·Î½ ÏÏÎ¿Î»Î¿Î³Î¯Î¶Î¿Î½ÏÎ±Î¹ Î¿Î¹ Î±Î¼ÏÎ½ÏÎ¹ÎºÎ­Ï Î´Î±ÏÎ¬Î½ÎµÏ ÏÏÎ¿ Î­Î»Î»ÎµÎ¹Î¼Î¼Î± ÏÏÎ½ ÏÏÏÏÎ½. ÎÏÏÏ ÎµÎ¯Î½Î±Î¹ ÏÎ¬ÏÎ± ÏÎ¿Î»Ï ÏÎ·Î¼Î±Î½ÏÎ¹ÎºÏ ÎºÎ±Î¹ Î¼Î¬Î»Î¹ÏÏÎ± Î¿ ÏÏÎ¿ÏÏÎ³ÏÏ ÎÎ¼ÏÎ½Î±Ï ÏÎ·Ï ÎÎ»Î»Î¬Î´Î¿Ï, Î¿ ÎºÏÏÎ¹Î¿Ï ÎÎ­Î½Î´Î¹Î±Ï, ÏÏÎ¹Ï 31 ÎÏÎ³Î¿ÏÏÏÎ¿Ï ÏÎ¿Ï 2024, Î´Î®Î»ÏÏÎµ ÏÏÎ·Î½ Î ÏÎ¬Î³Î± ÏÏÎ¹ ÏÏÎ­ÏÎµÎ¹ Î½Î± ÏÏÎ¬ÏÎ¾Î¿ÏÎ½ Î±Î»Î»Î±Î³Î­Ï, Î½Î± Î±Î»Î»Î¬Î¾Î¿ÏÎ½ Î¿Î¹ Î´Î·Î¼Î¿ÏÎ¹Î¿Î½Î¿Î¼Î¹ÎºÎ¿Î¯ ÎºÎ±Î½ÏÎ½ÎµÏ ÏÏÎ¿Î½ Î±ÏÎ¿ÏÎ¬ ÏÎ·Î½ Î¬Î¼ÏÎ½Î±. ÎÏÏÏ Î¸Î± Î®ÏÎ±Î½ ÏÎ¿ ÎºÏÏÎ¹ÏÏÎµÏÎ¿ ÏÎ¿Ï Î¸Î± ÏÏÎ­ÏÎµÎ¹ Î½Î± ÎºÎ¬Î½ÎµÎ¹ Î· ÎÏÏÏÏÎ±ÏÎºÎ® ÎÎ½ÏÏÎ·.Â ÎÎ»ÎµÎ¯Î½Î¿Î½ÏÎ±Ï, Î· ÎºÎ¯Î½Î·ÏÎ· Î±ÏÏÎ®, ÎºÎ±ÏÎ¬ ÏÎ¿ÏÏ ÎµÎ¹Î´Î¹ÎºÎ¿ÏÏ, ÎºÏÏÎ¹Îµ ÎÏÎ¯ÏÏÎ¿ÏÎµ, ÎºÎ±Î¹ ÏÎ·Î¼ÎµÎ¹ÏÏÏÎµ ÏÎ¿ Î±ÏÏÏ, Î¸Î± Î±ÏÎ¿ÏÎµÎ»Î­ÏÎµÎ¹ Î´Î¹Î±ÏÎºÎ® Î¿Î¹ÎºÎ¿Î½Î¿Î¼Î¹ÎºÎ® Î±ÏÏÎ¯Î´Î± ÏÏÎ¹Ï Î¿Î¹ÎºÎ¿Î½Î¿Î¼Î¯ÎµÏ ÎºÎ±Î¹ Î¸Î± Î»ÎµÎ¹ÏÎ¿ÏÏÎ³Î®ÏÎµÎ¹ ÏÏÎ¿Ï ÏÏÎµÎ»Î¿Ï ÏÏÎ½ ÎºÏÎ±ÏÏÎ½ Î³Î¹Î± ÎºÎ¿Î¹Î½ÏÎ½Î¹ÎºÎ­Ï ÎµÎ»Î±ÏÏÏÎ½ÏÎµÎ¹Ï.

Â Â Evin Incir (S&D). â Madam President, colleagues, the EU annual budget plays a vital role in upholding our commitment to global humanitarian efforts. One crucial partner in this is UNWRA, the backbone of the humanitarian response in Gaza. We must continue supporting it, especially as Israel continues its military attack against civilians, health and humanitarian staff, journalists and hospitals.

Unfortunately, there are sustained disinformation campaigns and blunt lies against UNWRA, even here in our Parliament. The extreme right, but also, unfortunately, some liberals â I'm referring to the previous colleague from Renew â have taken a one-sided view of the conflict, ignoring war crimes and crimes against humanity in Gaza. We cannot allow such extremist actors to shape our policies.

I therefore urge all colleagues here today to vote against AmendmentsÂ 1, 36 and 69, which are attempts at undermining UNWRA and the Palestinian Authority. I urge all colleagues to stand on the right side of history, by saving lives and not being complicit in the current ongoing horror.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Moritz KÃ¶rner (Renew), blue-card question. â Colleague, thank you for accepting my blue card. Because you referred directly to me, I want to ask where in my speech I talked about the conflict between Israel and Palestine. I didn't talk about UNWRA. I also didn't talk about the Palestinian Authority. I just talked about antisemitic schoolbooks.

And I want to ask you: who would be against removing antisemitic content from school books, other than an anti-Semite?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Evin Incir (S&D), blue-card answer. â Colleagues, everybody knows that when referring to the textbooks, the person who is doing that is trying to undermine the work of UNWRA. This is co-related in the discussion, and it's been done year after year.

When it comes to the textbooks, we have had the Georg Eckert Institute report, done by the EU, which has said that the Palestinian textbooks adhere to UNESCO standards, but that there are still some problems within them. Of course, those problems need to be addressed. I totally agree with you. Hatred doesn't have a place in any textbook. But it doesn't do it in the Israeli textbooks either, because in Israeli textbooks, Palestine does not exist.

So I agree with you, but I would say that we should demand the same from the Palestinians and the Israelis.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Patryk Jaki (ECR). â Pani PrzewodniczÄca! Kiedy Polska wchodziÅa do Unii Europejskiej wzrost gospodarczy Unii byÅ podobny do StanÃ³w Zjednoczonych. Potem wprowadziliÅcie ETS. Samo wasze sprawozdanie, sprawozdanie Draghiego wskazuje na dwa gÅÃ³wne powody.

Po pierwsze: przeregulowanie. Ta hiperinflacja prawa powoduje, Å¼e odnaleÅºÄ siÄ w niej potrafiÄ tylko giganci i odbywa siÄ to kosztem konkurencyjnoÅci maÅych i Årednich firm. I po drugie: ceny energii obÅoÅ¼one absurdalnymi, ideologicznymi podatkami, przez ktÃ³re europejskie firmy przestaÅy siÄ liczyÄ.

No i wreszcie ideologia, gdzie ÅmierÄ w postaci aborcji czy eutanazji jest bardziej poÅ¼Ädana niÅ¼ Å¼ycie, a dzieci sÄ Åºle postrzegane, bo rzekomo produkujÄ za duÅ¼o dwutlenku wÄgla. I przez to musicie siÄgaÄ po migracje i ÅciÄgaÄ coraz wiÄcej ludzi do Unii Europejskiej, ktÃ³rzy generujÄ tylko koszty i problemy. WiÄc ten budÅ¼et jest tym, co spowodowaÅo, Å¼e kaÅ¼dego roku Europa jest coraz sÅabsza. I albo to siÄ zmieni, albo spowoduje to gigantycznÄ katastrofÄ.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Anouk Van Brug (Renew). â Voorzitter, het is tijd om onze ogen te openen voor de harde realiteit. Onze veiligheid staat onder druk en economische voorspoed is niet meer vanzelfsprekend.

Dit vraagt om een begroting die klaar is voor de toekomst. We kunnen niet langer tweederde van onze begroting uitgeven aan landbouw en cohesie. We moeten stevig investeren in defensie, grip krijgen op migratie en innovatie stimuleren. Dit zijn de echte uitdagingen van vandaag.

Laten we samen een begroting maken die niet alleen ons continent veilig houdt, maar ook zorgt voor groene groei en een sterke positie in de wereld. Samen kunnen we bouwen aan een betere toekomst.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Marcos Ros Sempere (S&D). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, la UniÃ³n Europea es una gran maquinaria y para avanzar necesita energÃ­a. El presupuesto que hoy debatimos es fundamental: Â¿hacia dÃ³nde queremos avanzar?, Â¿en quÃ© medidas invertimos mÃ¡s esfuerzo para lograr nuestros objetivos? Y ese es el debate. Hay quienes hablan solo de gasto, pero solo quieren manchar de negatividad el enorme trabajo de la UniÃ³n Europea, un trabajo de gran valor, por ejemplo, en diversidad de Ã¡mbitos, como puede ser nuestros entornos urbanos.

La Nueva Bauhaus Europea sigue creciendo en todos los paÃ­ses, y me siento muy orgulloso de que el proyecto piloto que la impulsa siga considerado en este presupuesto porque necesitamos hacer esfuerzo presupuestario en todos los Ã¡mbitos de la vivienda y el urbanismo y en los energÃ©ticamente sostenibles: el camino de la Nueva Bauhaus Europea. El debate sobre los presupuestos determina hacia dÃ³nde queremos que avance la maquinaria, por eso me preocupa el largo plazo, no solo el presupuesto de 2025, propuestas que llegan y que abogan por que la UniÃ³n Europea se salga de su camino: permanezcamos unidos en la diversidad apostando por un presupuesto europeo mÃ¡s fuerte.

ÎÎ´Î¹Î±Î¯ÏÎµÏÎ± ÏÎ·Î¼Î±Î½ÏÎ¹ÎºÎ® ÎµÎ¯Î½Î±Î¹ ÎºÎ±Î¹ Î· ÏÎµÏÎ±Î¹ÏÎ­ÏÏ ÏÏÎ®ÏÎ¹Î¾Î· ÏÏÎ½ Î±Î³ÏÎ¿ÏÏÎ½ Î¼Î±Ï Î¼Îµ ÎµÏÎ¹ÏÎ»Î­Î¿Î½ 40 ÎµÎºÎ±ÏÎ¿Î¼Î¼ÏÏÎ¹Î± ÎµÏÏÏ Î³Î¹Î± ÏÎ·Î½ ÎµÎ½Î¯ÏÏÏÏÎ· ÏÏÎ½ Î½Î­ÏÎ½ Î³ÎµÏÏÎ³ÏÎ½ ÎºÎ±Î¹ 25 ÎµÎºÎ±ÏÎ¿Î¼Î¼ÏÏÎ¹Î± ÎµÏÏÏ Î³Î¹Î± ÏÎ·Î½ ÏÏÎ¿ÏÎ¸Î·ÏÎ· ÏÏÎ½ Î³ÎµÏÏÎ³Î¹ÎºÏÎ½ ÏÏÎ¿ÏÏÎ½ÏÏÎ½.Â Î Î¹ÏÏÎµÏÎ¿ÏÎ¼Îµ ÏÎµ Î¼Î¹Î± Î¹ÏÏÏÏÎ® ÎÏÏÏÏÎ±ÏÎºÎ® ÎÎ½ÏÏÎ·Â· ÎºÎ±Î¹ Î¼Î¹Î± ÎÏÏÏÏÎ±ÏÎºÎ® ÎÎ½ÏÏÎ· ÎµÎ¯Î½Î±Î¹ Î¹ÏÏÏÏÎ® ÏÏÎ±Î½ ÏÏÎ­ÎºÎµÏÎ±Î¹ Î´Î¯ÏÎ»Î± ÏÏÎ¿ÏÏ Î±Î½Î¸ÏÏÏÎ¿ÏÏ ÏÎ·Ï.

Â Â Nicola Zingaretti (S&D). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, se esistesse un tribunale dei diritti dei giovani, condannerebbe i tagli al bilancio europeo.

In questo luogo, il Parlamento, abbiamo il dovere di portare i nostri valori, le nostre idee, anche di difendere gli interessi della nostra gente, che coincidono con un'Europa piÃ¹ forte e piÃ¹ umana. PerchÃ© le persone hanno paura, chiedono sicurezza, speranza e â da solo â nessun Paese europeo ce la farÃ  mai a garantirle, perchÃ© non resisterebbe alla violenza della competizione globale.

Per questo i tagli previsti da governi, in gran parte di destra, sono sbagliati e sono tagli alla speranza. Ã sbagliato tagliare sulla ricerca, sulla sanitÃ  o su Erasmus, come invece Ã¨ stato proposto. Non sono risparmi: sono rinunce che colpiranno in modo particolare le ragazze e i ragazzi.

Lo denunciamo oggi e continueremo a farlo ogni volta che sarÃ  necessario.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â JÃ¼ri Ratas (PPE). â Madam President, dear colleagues, recently the need to improve Europe's competitiveness has been a central topic. Even Draghi's report has highlighted the urgent need for Europe to strengthen its position in the global economy. If we are serious about this goal, we must prioritise investment in research, innovation and technological development. The EU budget for 2025 must reflect this commitment. Our ability to keep pace with global leaders like the United States and China depends on it.

The counterproposal to reduce research funding by EURÂ 450Â million is not sustainable. It threatens our innovative capacity and also weakens our competitiveness. By investing in science, we can provide our industries the opportunity to take the lead in high-value sectors and create new jobs. I believe we should support the increase in research and development funding to 3Â % of GDP.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Vytenis Povilas Andriukaitis (S&D). â Gerbiama posÄdÅ¾io pirmininke, gerbiamas Komisare, gerbiamas Ministre, Europos Tarybos pateiktas projektas, deja, neatitinka prioritetÅ³ ir tie sumaÅ¾inimai sveikatos apsaugai, âHorizonâ programai, âYouth4Healthâ programai yra neatitinkantys iÅ¡ esmÄs investicinÄs politikos, nes tai yra investicinÄs politikos dalis, ir dÄl to dÄkoju Victorui Negrescu ir Europos Parlamento visiems dirbusiems, kurie pasiÅ«lÄ projektÄ kompromisinÄ¯ ir pasiÅ«lÄ padidinimus bÅ«tent. Ir aÅ¡ norÄÄiau pasidÅ¾iaugti Victoro Negrescu sÄkme ir taip pat noriu pasakyti, kad Europos Komisija teisingai sako, kad ji palaikys Europos Parlamento siÅ«lymÄ padidinti iÅ¡laidas, padidinti investicijas Ä¯ tai, kas yra pasiÅ«lyta. Suprantama, tai yra kompromisinis projektas ir mes turime kuo skubiau jÄ¯ patvirtinti, bet, kolegos, didÅ¾iausia problema aplamai lieka, kad Europos Taryba iki Å¡iol nÄra patvirtinusi pastoviÅ³ nuosavÅ³jÅ³ iÅ¡tekliÅ³. Be pastoviÅ³ nuosavÅ³jÅ³ iÅ¡tekliÅ³ judÄti toliau ateityje negalime. Europos SÄjungos biudÅ¾etas aplamai yra per maÅ¾as. Jeigu lyginti su visÅ³ Å¡aliÅ³ nariÅ³ biudÅ¾etais ir jeigu mes Å¾iÅ«rim Ä¯ priekÄ¯ apie plÄtrÄ, apie tai, kad reikÄs priimti 10 naujÅ³ nariÅ³, kaip gi galima su tokiu maÅ¾u Europos SÄjungos biudÅ¾etu galvoti apie perspektyvas? Akivaizdu, kad mums reikia ir Lisabonos sutarties perÅ¾iÅ«rÄ skatinti, stimuliuoti debatus apie ateitÄ¯. Kitaip mes tikrai nepasieksime savo tikslÅ³.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Salvatore De Meo (PPE). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, il bilancio del 2025 Ã¨ fondamentale per affrontare le sfide che ci attendono e siamo tutti consapevoli della necessitÃ  di dover utilizzare queste risorse in modo appropriato e funzionale rispetto alle nostre strategie.

Sicuramente la politica agricola si conferma tra le nostre prioritÃ , per aumentare la competitivitÃ  e la sicurezza e autonomia alimentare dell'Europa.

Certo, la nostra architettura finanziaria e le nostre risorse economiche non sono sufficienti rispetto a quelle che sono le nostre sfide. Ecco perchÃ© necessiterebbe il tutto di essere rivisto, magari ricorrendo al debito comune, per avere piÃ¹ risorse e favorire maggiormente il raggiungimento degli obiettivi.

Ad ogni modo, i 60 miliardi circa di euro destinati alla PAC continuano ad essere un'opportunitÃ  fondamentale per sostenere i nostri agricoltori, affinchÃ© siano ovviamente impegnati nella transizione verde.

Sono risorse destinate a promuovere pratiche agricole sostenibili, la ricerca e l'utilizzo delle tecnologie innovative. Ma soprattutto sono risorse con cui i nostri agricoltori vogliono essere protagonisti nella lotta ai cambiamenti climatici, perchÃ© sono alleati dell'ambiente e non avversari.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â TomÃ¡Å¡ ZdechovskÃ½ (PPE). â PanÃ­ pÅedsedajÃ­cÃ­, evropskÃ½ rozpoÄet je nejvÄtÅ¡Ã­ investiÄnÃ­ nÃ¡stroj, jakÃ½ mÃ¡me. My se musÃ­me nauÄit pracovat s rozpoÄtem tak, aby byly investice efektivnÃ­, sprÃ¡vnÄ zacÃ­lenÃ© a s co nejvÄtÅ¡Ã­m dopadem. DÃ¡my a pÃ¡novÃ©, rozpoÄet musÃ­ podporovat rÅ¯st a vytvÃ¡Ået konkurenceschopnÃ¡ pracovnÃ­ mÃ­sta. Evropa musÃ­ bÃ½t globÃ¡lnÄ prvnÃ­, ne naopak. UÅ¾ si nesmÃ­me sami hÃ¡zet klacky pod nohy a pomÃ¡hat SpojenÃ½m stÃ¡tÅ¯m a ÄÃ­nÄ udrÅ¾et se na svÄtovÃ© Å¡piÄce. V rozpoÄtu se musÃ­ vÃ­ce projevit Draghiho zprÃ¡va. MusÃ­me vÃ­ce investovat do podpory vÃ½zkumu a inovacÃ­. StejnÄ tak i efektivnÃ­ ÅÃ­zenÃ­ migrace a hraniÄnÃ­ infrastruktura, to vÅ¡echno musÃ­ bÃ½t naÅ¡e priority nejen na rok 2025, ale i do dalÅ¡Ã­ch let. Zcela zÃ¡sadnÃ­ je i obrana, a to jak produkce, tak i vÃ½zkum a vÃ½voj. Investice do tÄchto poloÅ¾ek rozhodnou o budoucnosti Evropy a jÃ¡ chci, aby Evropa byla na Å¡piÄce, ne v jejÃ­m zÃ¡vÄsu.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

Procedura "catch-the-eye"

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Anna StÃ¼rgkh (Renew). â MadamÂ President, any young person whose life has been enriched by living, studying or working abroad, whose horizon has been broadened by immersing themselves in a foreign culture or foreign language, any young person who's been able to make Europe their home through the Erasmus programme knows that this is one of the greatest achievements of the European Union. So why doesn't the Council know?

Erasmus is so much more than a simple exchange programme. Erasmus has increased national GDPs. Erasmus has decreased unemployment rates. Erasmus has fathered quite literally more than 1Â million babies. So why do nation states want to cut its budget?

Dear Council, keep your hands off Erasmus, for the European youth and for the European future.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marc Botenga (The Left). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, je veux quand mÃªme rÃ©agir Ã  la perfidie de certaines propositions formulÃ©es ici.

Certains collÃ¨gues voient IsraÃ«l effectuer un gÃ©nocide Ã  Gaza, un nettoyage ethnique en Cisjordanie, ils voient IsraÃ«l attaquer le Liban et, malgrÃ© tout cela, ils ne demandent pas de sanctions, ils continuent de soutenir les livraisons dâarmes Ã  Netanyahou, rendant par lÃ  mÃªme lâUnion europÃ©enne complice de crimes de guerre.

Aujourdâhui ces mÃªmes collÃ¨gues proposent, aprÃ¨s plus de un an de gÃ©nocide, de sanctionner les Palestiniens, de sanctionner les Nations unies, de demander Ã  ce que lâon retire les fonds de lâAutoritÃ© palestinienne et que lâon sâen prenne Ã  lâagence qui gÃ¨re les rÃ©fugiÃ©s dans cinq pays.

Non mais vous nâavez pas honte? Vous nâavez plus aucune limite! Alors quoi, mÃªme lâaide humanitaire est un problÃ¨me? Vous voulez vraiment aider IsraÃ«l Ã  parachever le nettoyage ethnique de la Palestine? Ayez honte, franchement, de venir ici avec ce genre de proposition, avec ce genre dâamendement! Cela ne doit pas passer!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lukas Sieper (NI). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, liebe Mitmenschen! In dieser Generaldebatte Ã¼ber den Haushalt der EU fÃ¼r das Jahr 2025 mÃ¶chte ich als letzter auch noch einmal Ihr Augenmerk auf einen Aspekt legen, der hier zum GlÃ¼ck schon Ã¶fter angesprochen wurde: das Erasmus+-Programm. Dieses Leuchtturmprojekt der EU ermÃ¶glicht es jungen Menschen in Ausbildung und Studium, Lehrpersonal und allgemeinen Verwaltungspersonen, den Schritt nach Europa zu machen. Es ist Grundpfeiler von Erhalt und Weiterentwicklung der europÃ¤ischen Idee.

Und deshalbÂ â nicht nur, weil ich selbst mein Erasmus in Amsterdam geliebt habe oder meine Freundin ihr Erasmus in Paris gerade liebtÂ â kann ich nicht verstehen, wieso Rat und Kommission dieses Budget fÃ¼r Erasmus+ im kommenden Jahr kÃ¼rzen wollen. In diesen Zeiten von Wirtschaftskrise, Krieg, Bedrohung der Demokratie und KlimawandelÂ â was bleibt uns, auÃer in eine bessere Zukunft zu investieren? Bildung ist die beste Investition in diese bessere Zukunft.

Lassen Sie uns nicht daran sparen, lassen Sie uns nicht am Erasmus+-Programm sparen!

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(Fine della procedura "catch the eye")

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Wopke Hoekstra, Member of the Commission. â Madam President, ladies and gentlemen, on behalf of my dear friend and dear colleague Commissioner Hahn, thank you for today's debate. We carefully note the positions expressed by this House and, of course, by the Council.

To facilitate the upcoming negotiations, the Commission will present an assessment of all the amendments proposed by Parliament and of the Council's position in the so-called 'letter of executability'. We will have the opportunity to discuss again on 5Â November, when we meet in the Conciliation Committee format. And we believe that successful negotiations require both institutions to set clear priorities so that we can focus our discussions in conciliation on those programmes where meaningful adjustments actually could be made.

The Commission, as always, will act as an honest broker and our services will do their utmost to prepare the ground for fair and informed negotiations. We, of course, in particular Commissioner Hahn, look forward to having constructive negotiations to reach a timely agreement on making this annual budget also a quality budget.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â PÃ©ter BenÅ Banai, President-in-Office of the Council. â MadamÂ President, honourable Members, I have listened carefully to the arguments raised during this interesting debate, and I will inform my colleagues in the Council accordingly. Not all, but most of the suggestions I have heard were on increasing expenditure in various areas. Let me mention three points here.

The first one: I haven't seen the budget of a country, nor of the European Union, nor of any international agreements, where all issues, all expenditure items, could be financed as priority items. We have to distinguish clearly between more and less important issues. As I see, the position of the Parliament and the Council is quite close to each other as regards cohesion, agriculture, border protection, humanitarian aid and defence. And of course there are some differences.

The second point: unfortunately, I haven't heard about the source of additional financing. It's not the Member States but the European taxpayers who pay the additional cost of additional expenditures. Do we want to increase taxes? Do we want to increase the indebtedness of the European Union? Just look at the 2025 budget proposal. Interest expenditures for NextGenerationEU should be something like double the original amount. Something like an additional EURÂ 2.5Â billion we have to find to pay the interest costs.

Point three: the Council proposal does not include cuts as regards the total sum. On the contrary, if I compare the proposal made by the Council on the 2025 EU budget, that includes an increase in the total sum as regards both commitments and payments. What we would like to avoid is the nonâjustified, nonâunderpinned increase. And this is true as regards the case of Erasmus+. And we would like to stress here that the draft budget included additional funds originating from the previous MFF period and which was not associated with the current financial programming. This is why the Council has suggested keeping the appropriations of the programme at the level of its financial programming. This way, we can assure you that no originally planned demands for 2025 are taken away, and no students will be harmed by this adjustment.

The Council will do its utmost to engage in a constructive dialogue, allowing us to come to an agreement on a budget for 2025 within the deadlines foreseen by the Treaty.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Victor Negrescu, rapporteur. â Madam President, thank you, and thank you, Minister and Commissioner, I have listened carefully to all the points of views expressed, and I respect all positions. I'm even more grateful for the strong support expressed in the BUDG Committee for the report and the resolution.

The EU budget maze is already complicated enough, and I am happy with the balanced language we have managed to reach on the most complex issues, including on ensuring EU funds are not used to finance terrorism, border protection or the situation in the Middle East. I am particularly happy with the strong language that we have in relation to the fight against corruption. Any amendments that go against these fragile, but strong, compromises will hurt the European Parliament and our capacity to actually make our priorities heard.

With regard to the Council position, we understand the challenges faced by our Member States, but cutting the EU budget would actually accelerate our budgetary and financial challenges, because everyone knows that the euros we have invested at EU level have a multiplication effect that benefits everyone. If we want the German economy to recover or the French deficit to be resolved, we also need to invest in our economy, our competitiveness and in our common development.

I'm confident we will move forward and find a good compromise in a timely manner. We will also count on the Commission to act as an honest broker. I'm confident that after the votes tomorrow, the European Parliament will enter negotiations with a clear and ambitious position that will allow us to work together in building a 2025 EU budget that matches citizens' expectations.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Niclas Herbst, Berichterstatter. â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, meine Damen und Herren! Vielen Dank fÃ¼r die Debatte. Die vielen Wortmeldungen haben mir gezeigt, dass es sehr weitgehende WÃ¼nsche gibt, aus den FachausschÃ¼ssen, von den Kolleginnen und Kollegen, und wir haben, glaube ich, auch gemerkt, dass die komplizierte Verfasstheit unseres Haushalts dazu fÃ¼hrt, dass wir die notwendige FlexibilitÃ¤t nur sehr begrenzt haben. Die Debatte hat ja auch den zukÃ¼nftigen MFR angesprochen. Ich glaube, wir mÃ¼ssen gemeinsam daran arbeiten, diese FlexibilitÃ¤t zu erreichen, um das dann auch stÃ¤rker politisch umsetzen zu kÃ¶nnen.

Mir ist wichtig, dass deutlich geworden ist, dass Ã¶ffentliche Ausgaben auch im Personalwesen durchaus ihren Sinn haben kÃ¶nnen, dass wir dort sehr zurÃ¼ckhaltend sein mÃ¼ssen, dass wir mit dem Geld der Steuerzahlerinnen und Steuerzahler sehr sinnvoll umgehen mÃ¼ssen, aber dass wir auch in der Lage sein mÃ¼ssen, Dinge durchzusetzen.

Ich finde es gut, dass, anders als in den Vorjahren, andere EinzelplÃ¤ne oder RubrikÂ 7 keine groÃe Rolle gespielt haben, weil uns in der TatÂ â Sie haben Ã¼ber die Priorisierung gesprochenÂ â vÃ¶llig klar sein muss, dass wir uns auf die wichtigen Dinge konzentrieren mÃ¼ssen und dort auch keine NebenkriegsschauplÃ¤tze haben dÃ¼rfen.

Ich glaube, dass, wenn wir es schaffen, uns auf die wichtigen Themen zu konzentrieren, wir es trotz der komplizierten Ausgangslage schaffen kÃ¶nnen, ein gutes Ergebnis fÃ¼r die Menschen in Europa zu erzielen. Ich freue mich auf die Verhandlungen, bedanke mich fÃ¼r die Debatte und bin Optimist, und das werde ich auch bis zum Ende bleiben.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Presidente. â La discussione Ã¨ chiusa.

La votazione si svolgerÃ  domani.

Â 

12. Protection of European journalists reporting on Russiaâs war of aggression against Ukraine (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â Presidente. â L'ordine del giorno reca in discussione la dichiarazione della Commissione sulla tutela dei giornalisti europei che coprono la guerra di aggressione della Russia contro l'Ucraina (2024/2895(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Wopke Hoekstra, Member of the Commission. â MadamÂ President, honourable Members of the European Parliament, let me start with something that you unfortunately already know: Russia lacks any respect for media freedom inside its own country, and it also blatantly disrespects the protection of journalists in conflict zones. Inside the country, the space for media has dramatically shrunk since the start of the fullâscale Russian invasion of Ukraine. Many media outlets were forced to terminate their operations. The majority of independent journalists have left the country and many are actually being persecuted. And it is this appalling crackdown on independent media that has also spilled over to the frontline and the territories of Ukraine temporarily â temporarily! â occupied by Russia. And it is there that Russia harasses, intimidates and uses violence against journalists and media workers.

And this is exactly what you think it is: a clear attempt to silence them, in order to not let the world receive full and impartial information about what is actually going on. They are trying to prevent the exposure of Russian stateâsponsored disinformation about the war they wage in Ukraine. According to Reporters Without Borders, more than 100 journalists have been victims of Russian crimes since the beginning of the fullâscale invasion. That includes at least 35 wounded, 12 detained, as well as 233 media outlets closed in the territories occupied by Russia.

Ladies and gentlemen, the appalling case of last week's death of a Ukrainian freelance journalist, Victoria Roshchyna, while in illegal, arbitrary Russian detention, is yet another example of Russia's utter disregard for media freedom and international commitments. Victoria Roshchyna was a renowned Ukrainian journalist covering Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine, and she was the recipient of the Courage in Journalism Award for her work, and was determined to bring the truth â the truth about Russia's brutal war of aggression and its illegal occupation of Ukraine â to the world. That is what she was doing. And according to the available information, Victoria died while being transferred to Moscow from a prison in Taganrog. And it is the Russian authorities, and noÂ one else, who, by doing so, assumed full responsibility for her safety, for her health and physical integrity when they arbitrarily detained her in the temporarily occupied Ukrainian territories in August 2023.

Ladies and gentlemen, I'm going to make it worse: she is the 13th murdered journalist since the start of Russia's fullâscale invasion of Ukraine, and it is absolutely outrageous. Lately, the Russian Federal Security Service decided to initiate criminal cases against journalists covering Ukraine's counteroffensive operation in Kursk. And now, already 14 journalists are subject to such utterly ridiculous charges. It is again a clear attempt to intimidate journalists and to discourage them from performing their professional duties. We cannot allow that.

Honourable Members, the European Union's position is straightforward: journalists and media workers alike play an indispensable role in providing accurate and timely coverage from war zones, ensuring that information about the realities and impact of war can actually reach a global audience, including us. Journalists are considered civilians under international humanitarian law and should be actively protected in all conflict situations. The EU has and will condemn any form of Russia's ongoing harassment, intimidation and violence against journalists and media workers covering war zones and frontlines. For them, safety and protection must be ensured at all times. There can be no impunity, and I say that again: there can and will be no impunity for human rights violations and abuses against journalists.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Isabel Wiseler-Lima, au nom du groupe PPE. â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire, merci pour vos paroles. Elle sâappelait Victoria Rochtchyna, elle avait 27Â ans, elle Ã©tait journaliste, elle Ã©tait ukrainienne. Elle a disparu le 3Â aoÃ»t, alors quâelle se trouvait en Russie pour enquÃªter sur les frappes menÃ©es par lâarmÃ©e russe contre lâUkraine. Quelque 25Â journalistes ukrainiens se trouvent aujourdâhui emprisonnÃ©s en Russie. Victoria Rochtchyna Ã©tait elle aussi enfermÃ©e dans une prison russe. Et câest lÃ  quâelle est dÃ©cÃ©dÃ©e.

Les journalistes indÃ©pendants Ã  la recherche des faits sont particuliÃ¨rement haÃ¯s des tyrans, car ils sont dangereux pour les rÃ©gimes qui imposent une vision tronquÃ©e, mensongÃ¨re de la rÃ©alitÃ©. Les journalistes sont des piliers de la dÃ©mocratie. Ils Ã©clairent les gens, relatent les faits, dÃ©mystifient les mensonges et dÃ©noncent la corruption et les abus de pouvoir. Le travail dâinformation âÂ leur travail dâinformationÂ â nous est indispensable.

Le gouvernement russe nâhÃ©site pas Ã  emprisonner des journalistes qui ne font que leur mÃ©tier, honnÃªtement. Dâailleurs, tout comme dâautres simples civils ukrainiens, eux aussi sont nombreux Ã  avoir disparu dans les geÃ´les russes.

Je ne peux terminer cette intervention sans mentionner tous les enfants ukrainiens dÃ©portÃ©s en Russie. Il est grand temps que le monde occidental fasse bien plus pour aider lâUkraine Ã  gagner cette guerre.

ChÃ¨re famille de Victoria, nous vous prÃ©sentons nos trÃ¨s sincÃ¨res condolÃ©ances et vous assurons que Victoria ne sera pas oubliÃ©e. Nous appelons Ã  ce que les circonstances de son dÃ©cÃ¨s soient Ã©lucidÃ©es.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sandro Ruotolo, a nome del gruppo S&D. â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, come delegazione italiana del gruppo dei Socialisti e Democratici al Parlamento europeo, abbiamo sollecitato il dibattito sulla protezione dei giornalisti che raccontano la guerra in Ucraina.

La decisione del governo russo di emettere i mandati di cattura nei confronti di due giornalisti italiani, Stefania Battistini e Simone Traini, Ã¨ gravissima: informare non Ã¨ mai un crimine. I due, accusati di attraversamento illegale del confine e riprese non autorizzate, rischiano fino a cinque anni di reclusione. Queste accuse violano la convenzione di Ginevra, minando la libertÃ  di stampa e la trasparenza. Non Ã¨ un caso isolato: altri giornalisti stranieri si trovano nella stessa situazione. Questo Ã¨ un chiaro tentativo di intimidazione che condanniamo con grande fermezza.

Quando i servizi segreti russi entrano in campo, occorre vigilare. La Russia ha intensificato la repressione del giornalismo indipendente, definendo i media europei e internazionali "agenti segreti". L'Europa deve farsi sentire, esprimendo solidarietÃ  ai giornalisti nel mirino di Putin, ma anche con azioni concrete: dobbiamo pretendere la revoca dei mandati di arresto per questi giornalisti. Chi vi parla Ã¨ uno dei 22 giornalisti minacciati che l'Italia protegge.

Voglio concludere facendo un appello a tutti i 27 Stati membri dell'Unione europea: proteggete i vostri giornalisti quando sono minacciati! La democrazia ha bisogno della libera informazione e quando muore un giornalista per il suo lavoro, non solo nei teatri di guerra ma anche nei Paesi cosiddetti "di pace", democratici, come in Europa, Ã¨ un pezzo di democrazia che muore.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Juan Carlos Girauta Vidal, en nombre del Grupo PfE. â SeÃ±ora presidente, es urgente, sÃ­, proteger a los periodistas que se juegan la vida cubriendo la guerra de agresiÃ³n de Rusia contra Ucrania: las muertes y agresiones sufridas por periodistas en el frente son inadmisibles y este Parlamento debe exigir mÃ¡s protecciÃ³n para quienes arriesgan su vida por informarnos.

Tampoco puedo dejar de mencionar el caso del espÃ­a PÃ¡vel AleksÃ©yevich Rubtsov, alias Pablo GonzÃ¡lez: durante aÃ±os, la izquierda en este Parlamento se dedicÃ³ a defenderlo acusando a Polonia de retener a un inocente periodista; hoy sabemos la verdad: Pavel ha sido reconocido y condecorado por Putin, confirmÃ¡ndose que trabajaba para el Kremlin. Ahora, los que alzaban la voz contra el Gobierno del PiS en Polonia guardan un sospechoso silencio.

PÃ¡vel, alias Pablo GonzÃ¡lez, trabajaba en La Sexta, una cadena de televisiÃ³n en EspaÃ±a conocida por su apoyo incondicional a Pedro SÃ¡nchez y su blanqueamiento del golpismo separatista del 1 de octubre de 2017, un golpe en el que tambiÃ©n estuvo implicada Rusia: nada de esto es una coincidencia.

Nuestros amigos del PiS han sido demonizados en esta CÃ¡mara, pero su Gobierno es el que mÃ¡s ha luchado contra la agresiÃ³n rusa: merecen nuestro respeto, no nuestra crÃ­tica. Aprendamos a distinguir a nuestros verdaderos aliados en la defensa de la libertad y de la democracia.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â MaÅgorzata Gosiewska, w imieniu grupy ECR. â Pani PrzewodniczÄca! O tym, jak waÅ¼nÄ i niebezpiecznÄ pracÄ wykonujÄ dziennikarze relacjonujÄcy przebieg rosyjskiej wojny przeciwko Ukrainie, nie muszÄ PaÅstwa przekonywaÄ. SÅuÅ¼Ä prawdzie i za prawdÄ przychodzi im ponosiÄ wysokÄ cenÄ, czasami najwyÅ¼szÄ. ChciaÅabym oddaÄ szczegÃ³lny hoÅd Wiktorii Roszczynie, 28-letniej ukraiÅskiej dziennikarce. Porwana i przetrzymywana przez Rosjan w jednym z najbrutalniejszych aresztÃ³w. ByÅa na liÅcie do wymiany. Nie doczekaÅa. Na Ukrainie ruszyÅo Åledztwo w sprawie zbrodni wojennej i morderstwa z premedytacjÄ â kolejnej rosyjskiej zbrodni.

Nie uchronimy dziennikarzy przed takim ryzykiem, ale moÅ¼emy utworzyÄ europejski fundusz, ktÃ³ry zapewniÅby stypendia i ubezpieczenie dla dziennikarzy pracujÄcych na terenach objÄtych wojnÄ oraz wsparcie dla ich rodzin. Ubezpieczenia w takich miejscach sÄ czÄsto niedostÄpne albo bardzo kosztowne. Funduszem takim mogÅyby zarzÄdzaÄ organizacje dziennikarskie z krajÃ³w przyfrontowych oraz z Ukrainy. Szanowni PaÅstwo, powoÅajmy fundusz imienia Viktorii Roszczyny. To bÄdzie konkret.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Helmut BrandstÃ¤tter, on behalf of the Renew Group. â Madam President, dear Commissioner, Thomas Jefferson, the third President of the United States, said, 'If I had to choose between a government without newspapers or newspapers without a government, I would prefer the latter'.

Freedom of media is the basis of democracy. Putin, of course, kills democracy. But as we see these days, in his country, in Ukraine and in other countries, he kills human beings; he kills journalists. There is an incomplete list of more than 100Â journalists who have been tortured or wounded or even killed. And why are they in Ukraine? Because they give us the possibility to understand what's happening there. They give us the possibility to learn the facts.Â As a lifelong journalist, I know that the most important job of a journalist is to report the facts so that the public can understand what's going on.

There are so many documentaries about what Putin and his soldiers are doing in Ukraine. We are very grateful to those wonderful journalists who told us these stories, and the sad story is that many of them were killed.

E io so, signora Presidente, che non si puÃ² mostrare un libro. Ma oggi io voglio mostrare alcune fotografie.

(The speaker showed photos of victims)

These are human beings. These are people we knew. These are people who worked for us. They are, of course, from Ukraine, but also from Russia. Oksana Baulina worked for Navalny and she was also killed because she wanted to show us the truth.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Presidente. â Non Ã¨ consentito mostrare le foto.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ville NiinistÃ¶, Verts/ALE-ryhmÃ¤n puolesta. â Arvoisa puhemies, demokratia ja vapaa yhteiskunta kuolevat pimeydessÃ¤. Vahva, riippumaton media ja sananvapaus ovat demokratian ydintÃ¤, ja ne takaavat sen, ettÃ¤ meidÃ¤n arvojamme puolustetaan nyt ja tulevaisuudessa. Toimittajien on pystyttÃ¤vÃ¤ tekemÃ¤Ã¤n tyÃ¶nsÃ¤ turvallisesti. Se on avoimen ja vapaan yhteiskunnan tiedonvÃ¤lityksen pohja, ja sitÃ¤ Putin myÃ¶s syvÃ¤sti pelkÃ¤Ã¤.

VenÃ¤jÃ¤n hyÃ¶kkÃ¤ykset niin VenÃ¤jÃ¤llÃ¤ kuin Ukrainan sotaan liittyen toimittajia ja median vapautta vastaan ovat tuomittavia. VenÃ¤jÃ¤n turvallisuuspalvelu FSB on nostanut syytteitÃ¤ kansainvÃ¤lisiÃ¤ toimittajia vastaan laittomasta maahantulosta. Toimittajat, muun muassa Deutsche WellestÃ¤ ja CNN:Ã¤ltÃ¤, ovat ylittÃ¤neet VenÃ¤jÃ¤n Ukrainan-rajan Sudzhassa Kurskin alueella LÃ¤nsi-VenÃ¤jÃ¤llÃ¤ raportoidessaan sodasta. Jos toimittajat todetaan syyllisiksi, he voivat saada jopa viiden vuoden vankeusrangaistuksen. On ilmeistÃ¤, ettÃ¤ syytteiden tarkoitus on hankaloittaa ja sensuroida sodasta raportoivien kansainvÃ¤listen toimittajien tyÃ¶tÃ¤, ja tÃ¤mÃ¤ on kansainvÃ¤lisen oikeuden vastaista.

Mutta VenÃ¤jÃ¤n toiminta mediaa vastaan ei rajoitu tÃ¤hÃ¤n. VenÃ¤jÃ¤n toimien ilmiselvÃ¤ tarkoitus on estÃ¤Ã¤ avoin raportointi sodasta ja VenÃ¤jÃ¤n tekemistÃ¤ sotarikoksista. Monet toimittajat, etenkin ukrainalaiset, ovat joutuneet vangituiksi tai jopa kuolleet VenÃ¤jÃ¤n vankeudessa ja kiduttamina, kuten viime kuussa kuollut Viktoriya Roshchyna, joka urhoollisesti toi esille ItÃ¤-Ukrainassa VenÃ¤jÃ¤n miehityksen alla tapahtuvan kÃ¤rsimyksen tosiasioita.

MeidÃ¤n Euroopassa on oltava tinkimÃ¤ttÃ¤ vapaan median puolella. MeidÃ¤n on pidettÃ¤vÃ¤ huoli siitÃ¤, ettÃ¤ vapaa media yllÃ¤pitÃ¤Ã¤ demokratiaa ja toivoa paremmasta tulevaisuudesta niin Ukrainassa kuin laajemmin Euroopassa.

Â Â Wopke Hoekstra, Member of the Commission. â MadamÂ President, thank you, and thank you very much, dear Members. Our approach towards Russia will continue to be crystal clear. We support Ukraine for as long as is necessary. We will isolate Russia. We will impose sanctions to hinder Russia's war machine. We will stand up for human rights and we will stand up for the press, for our journalists. Today's debate highlights the vital role of journalists and the role they play in war zones, including and covering Russia's invasion of Ukraine. And thank you for bringing more of the names of the brave men and women who have been there and who have been reporting to this debate.

We will also continue to call Russia out for its violations of international law. And we did so most recently at the OSCE, regarding Russia's ongoing intimidation and ongoing harassment of journalists in war zones and frontlines. And it was the High Representative's spokesman who issued a public statement on the death of Victoria in Russian captivity on 11Â October. In our view, it is absolutely essential that we have a thorough and independent investigation that fully clarifies all the circumstances of Victoria's death as soon as possible. And we will demand Russia to stop the abuse of its criminal system to intimidate journalists and other citizens by launching politically motivated criminal investigations against them.

In short, the European Union will remain steadfast in its commitment to protect media freedom and the safety of journalists, both at home and abroad.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Presidente. â Il processo verbale della seduta di lunedÃ¬ 21 ottobre 2024 e i testi approvati sono stati distribuiti.

Vi sono osservazioni? Non ci sono osservazioni.

Il processo verbale Ã¨ approvato.

15. Question Time with Commissioners â Situation of animal health in Europe: how to prevent and prepare for future sanitary crises in agriculture
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â Presidente. â L'ordine del giorno che reca il tempo delle interrogazioni alla Commissione, a norma dell'articolo 143 del nostro regolamento, dedicato al tema: "Situazione riguardante la salute degli animali: come prevenire le future crisi sanitari in agricoltura e prepararsi ad affrontarle".

Alcune informazioni per i colleghi. La durata prevista per il tempo delle interrogazioni Ã¨ di 90 minuti. Avete a disposizione un minuto per formulare l'interrogazione e la commissaria avrÃ  a disposizione due minuti per la risposta.

Per il primo turno di oratori sarÃ  possibile porre un'interrogazione supplementare di 30 secondi con due minuti per la risposta a disposizione della Commissaria.

Vi ricordo che l'eventuale interrogazione supplementare Ã¨ concessa soltanto se correlata all'interrogazione principale. Non consiste dunque in una nuova interrogazione.

Chi intende formulare l'interrogazione Ã¨ invitato a registrare sin d'ora la propria richiesta, utilizzando la funzione catch sul vostro dispositivo di voto. Dopo, ovviamente, aver inserito la vostra tessera di voto durante il tempo delle interrogazioni, i deputati interverranno dal proprio scranno e invito, naturalmente sin d'ora, a rispettare i tempi che ci siamo dati.

Siccome i deputati potrebbero aver bisogno di alcuni minuti per registrare la loro richiesta, Vi concederemo naturalmente questo tempo e Vi chiedo, come Vi anticipavo prima, di presentare sin d'ora la vostra richiesta, cosÃ¬ da poter procedere in maniera celere con i nostri lavori. Quindi, avete a disposizione qualche minuto di tempo per registrarVi, cosÃ¬ da iniziare subito.

Mi comunicano che l'onorevole Blaha aveva fatto richiesta di parlare per un fatto personale all'inizio della seduta.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

NeoliberÃ¡lny poslanec zÃ¡roveÅ poÅ¾iadal vedenie Parlamentu, aby ma potrestalo, lebo nedodrÅ¾ujem nejakÃº politickÃº lÃ­niu, ktorÃº si schvÃ¡lila rusofÃ³bna vÃ¤ÄÅ¡ina. Ako keby sme boli v Tretej rÃ­Å¡i. Ja som slobodnÃ½ Älovek, mÃ¡m slobodnÃ© nÃ¡zory a za svoje sÃºkromnÃ© peniaze budem cestovaÅ¥,kam len chcem. A ubezpeÄujem vÃ¡s, Å¾e som si v Ru u zo svojich peÅazÃ­ zaplatil vÅ¡etko do poslednÃ©ho borÅ¡Äu.Â MÃ¡m silnÃ½ mandÃ¡t od SlovÃ¡kov, aby som bojoval proti vojne a proti rusofÃ³bii. A mÃ¡m eÅ¡te silnejÅ¡Ã­ morÃ¡lny zÃ¡vÃ¤zok, Å¾e mÃ¡m tri malÃ© deti a nechcem, aby jednÃ©ho dÅa umierali v jadrovej vojne. A preto do Ruska pÃ´jdem znova. A mÃ´Å¾ete ma aj upÃ¡liÅ¥.
