ErrÅl szÃ³l ez a jelentÃ©s. Ãs igen, ÃnÃ¶k holnap arrÃ³l is szavazni fognak, hogy hogyan kÃ©pzelik el a mi kÃ¶zÃ¶s EurÃ³pÃ¡nkat. Vannak, akik Ãºgy gondoljÃ¡k, hogy EurÃ³pa csak egy gazdasÃ¡gi szÃ¶vetsÃ©g, akik Ãºgy tekintenek az eurÃ³pai emberekre, mint mondjuk erÅforrÃ¡sra, emberi erÅforrÃ¡sra, vagy Ã©ppen hiteladÃ³skÃ©nt, vagy maximum fogyasztÃ³kÃ©nt.

Mi itt, az EurÃ³pai Parlamentben, Ã©s nagyon remÃ©lem, hogy holnap a nagy tÃ¶bbsÃ©g azt fogja mondani, hogy az eurÃ³pai emberek hÃºs-vÃ©r emberek, hÃ©tkÃ¶znapi problÃ©mÃ¡kkal, akik nÃ©lkÃ¼l nincsen sikeres EurÃ³pa. EzÃ©rt Ã©n azt javaslom ÃnÃ¶knek, hogy ezt a jelentÃ©st fogadjÃ¡k el, hiszen az EurÃ³pai Parlament egyik legjobb arcÃ¡t mutatta a jelentÃ©s elkÃ©szÃ­tÃ©se kÃ¶zepette. KonzervatÃ­v, liberÃ¡lis, baloldali, zÃ¶ld, szociÃ¡ldemokrata kÃ©pviselÅk kÃ¶zÃ¶sen, sok vitÃ¡val Ã©s vÃ©gÃ¼l kompromisszummal fogadtÃ¡k ezt el. Ezt javaslom ÃnÃ¶knek is.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Nikola BartÅ¯Å¡ek, on behalf of the PfE Group. â MrÂ President, ladies and gentlemen, there is no doubt that employment policy is important, because it helps create job opportunities and promotes economic stability by reducing unemployment. Above all, it should be the competence of national states to ensure fair wages, protect workers' rights and address the specific needs of its labour market to reduce inequality. It is the Member States that should decide what is the best fit for their employment market.

On the one hand, we are opening pathways for migrants, who are coming to work here. And on the other hand, some Member States need to fund social benefits for unemployed citizens. This system is constantly lowering working conditions for European citizens. Above all else, due to this system, Europe is committing economic suicide. We are promoting green and digital jobs at the expense of regular jobs, and because of that, we are making Europe more vulnerable and dependent on foreign powers.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Chiara Gemma, a nome del gruppo ECR. â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario, sicuramente accogliamo con favore alcuni aggiornamenti integrati nei nuovi orientamenti per le politiche degli Stati membri a favore dell'occupazione.

I nostri emendamenti hanno rafforzato l'importanza dell'istruzione e riportato al centro il problema degli stipendi degli insegnanti, cosÃ¬ come il ruolo fondamentale della formazione professionale e del sostegno a persone anziane e con disabilitÃ .

Tuttavia, le criticitÃ  nel testo finale restano troppe: penso all'accesso al lavoro per le persone con disabilitÃ , che necessita di ulteriori sforzi per migliorare la loro indipendenza; penso al settore dell'istruzione, che soffre ancora di carenze croniche e di programmi scollegati dal mercato del lavoro; e poi nel testo rimangono troppi riferimenti ideologici al mainstreaming di genere e troppe richieste ridondanti di nuove proposte legislative da parte della Commissione.

Tutto questo, quando invece dovremmo concentrarci, ad esempio, sullo sviluppo delle competenze in Europa, evitando di ricorrere sistematicamente alla migrazione come soluzione alle carenze di manodopera.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jana Toom, on behalf of the Renew Group. â Mr President, colleagues, Commissioner, the European Semester is an important exercise. I welcome wholeheartedly the Commission's proposal to adjust the employment guidelines, and include the importance of social progress and the need to combat social exclusion and discrimination.

However, guidelines and recommendations, as good as they are, are not legally binding, and Member States have an ever dropping implementation rate when it comes to country-specific recommendations, only amounting to about 30Â %. While there is an increased rate of social recommendations, the implementation is even lower. Where the Commission has recommended social reforms, particularly those concerning poverty reduction, minimum income or labour market integration in previous years, it is clear that they have not been followed. That is therefore why the European Parliament also took the opportunity to stress the need for legislation, as the rapporteur already said.

There is a need for legislative initiatives on telework and the right to disconnect, prevention of work-related deaths, psychological risks, the framework directive on minimum income, setting standards on adequate safety nets and an increase of the child guarantee. And I very much hope that the next new Commission will deliver.

And as I have 10Â seconds, I have to say that it was quite shameful to listen that you managed to link this topic with migration.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Maria Ohisalo, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. â MrÂ President, dear colleagues, Commission, a just, green and social transition is the most critical challenge in our hands at the moment. It is imperative that employment measures support this huge transition. As the labour market is undergoing profound changes, we must ensure that new jobs that are created are quality jobs that enjoy the highest standards and labour rights.

Workers in a vulnerable situation cannot be left behind. Everyone has a right to safety at work and protection from dangerous conditions arising from, for example, climate change and loss of biodiversity, such as extreme weather events. Everyone has a right to social dialogue and collective bargaining, and to work in a place where artificial intelligence is used for their protection, not to undermine them.

All workers have a right to be treated with respect and dignity, without discrimination. All workers across the EU now have a right to a minimum wage. And I ask the Member States: why are you still not living up to this commonly agreed principle?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Rudi Kennes, namens de The Left-Fractie. â Voorzitter, we praten heel veel over een sociaal Europa. De Europese Unie legt BelgiÃ« echter deregulering op, wil het indexmechanisme afschaffen, dringt flexibiliteit op zonder deftige lonen, wil slechtere pensioenen en een hogere pensioenleeftijd. De Europese Unie doet niets om de toeleveranciers aan te pakken die werknemers in onzekere banen dwingen en zelfs een commissaris voor Werkgelegenheid en Sociale Zaken is er niet meer.

We moeten ons verzetten tegen de terugkerende bezuinigingen die in heel de EU verwoestingen hebben aangericht: jongeren die soms 2 tot 3Â nepjobs moeten combineren, gepensioneerden die moeten kiezen tussen verwarming of eten, vrouwen die moeten stoppen omdat er geen zorg meer is.

De door het Parlement voorgestelde ideeÃ«n gaan weliswaar een stuk in de goede richting, maar de vraag is wat er in de praktijk van gaat komen wanneer de EU pertinent enkel de belangen van de werkgevers blijft vertegenwoordigen. De Europese arbeidersklasse zal krijgen wat ze nodig heeft als ze verenigd is. Daarom roep ik alle Europese werknemer op zich aan te sluiten bij een vakbond zodat ze de goedbetaalde banen krijgen die ze verdienen.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Je veux vous parler dâune des filiÃ¨res qui perd le plus dâemplois dans mon pays. Je veux vous parler des agriculteurs franÃ§ais.

Ils travaillent plus durement que le plus travailleur dâentre vous et dans des conditions si inconfortables que vous ne les supporteriez pas plus dâune semaine. LevÃ©s avant lâaube, couchÃ©s aprÃ¨s la nuit, ils endurent tout. Depuis des siÃ¨cles, par amour de leur mÃ©tier, ils surmontent les intempÃ©ries, les blessures, la fatigue. Je sais que tous, ici, vous aimez la cuisine franÃ§aise, et pourtantâ¦ Pourtant, vous imposez aux gens qui rendent possible ce miracle des traitÃ©s de libre-Ã©change, qui sont systÃ©matiquement conclus Ã  lâavantage des pays Ã©trangers et au dÃ©triment de nos 400Â 000Â emplois agricoles. Vous leur imposez des normes innombrables, qui rendent leur mÃ©tier impossible. Oui, vous leur imposez ceci: un code rural franÃ§ais de 3Â 300Â pages. Lequel dâentre vous lâa lu, ce code? Pas un. Mais la majeure partie de ce code, qui les condamne, pour survivre, Ã  devenir experts-comptables, juristes, fonctionnaires et fiscalistes âÂ comme sâils nâavaient rien dâautre Ã  faireÂ â, elle a Ã©tÃ© votÃ©e ici.

Avant-hier, dans les Vosges, jâai rencontrÃ© Sophie, agricultrice. En Ã  peine un an, elle a subi, par votre faute, cinq contrÃ´les. Cinq humiliations, cinq pertes de temps âÂ donc dâargent, alors quâelle en a si peuÂ â, cinq angoisses, cinq preuves que la colÃ¨re des paysans, tout entiÃ¨re dirigÃ©e contre vous, est une colÃ¨re juste.

Avec ReconquÃªte!, je veux donner Ã  leur colÃ¨re un siÃ¨ge dans cet hÃ©micycle. Parce que ce matin, Ã  5Â heures, pendant que vous dormiez du sommeil du bureaucrate, des gens honnÃªtes et courageux se sont levÃ©s partout en France pour que vous puissiez manger. Tous les jours, ils travaillent pour vous. Alors demain, levez-vous Ã  5Â heures du matin et travaillez enfin pour eux.

Â Â Maravillas AbadÃ­a Jover (PPE). â SeÃ±or presidente, estas orientaciones representan una clara voluntad desde Europa de promover la sostenibilidad y el crecimiento, sin olvidarnos del pilar social. Sin embargo, en mi paÃ­s, en EspaÃ±a, los datos y recomendaciones que se presentarÃ¡n en el paquete de otoÃ±o indicarÃ¡n un estancamiento del empleo, especialmente entre los jÃ³venes.

Â Â Estelle Ceulemans (S&D). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, chers collÃ¨gues, le rapport sur lequel nous allons voter demain, et qui a Ã©tÃ© prÃ©sentÃ© par notre prÃ©sidente ici prÃ©sente, montre lâimportance de lâengagement du Parlement dans lâÃ©laboration des politiques de lâemploi. Les nombreux amendements qui ont Ã©tÃ© dÃ©posÃ©s, adoptÃ©s et soutenus par une large majoritÃ© de la commission de lâemploi et des affaires sociales montrent que lâengagement de ce Parlement doit Ãªtre entendu et respectÃ©.

Les amendements dÃ©posÃ©s se concentrent notamment sur les jeunes, et plus particuliÃ¨rement les NEET âÂ ni en emploi, ni en Ã©tudes, ni en formation, Ã¢gÃ©s de 15 Ã  25Â ans. Ces NEET sont passÃ©s, grÃ¢ce aux politiques europÃ©ennes, de 17Â % Ã  10Â % aujourdâhui. Malheureusement, 10Â %, cela reste beaucoup trop, puisque cela signifie que 1Â jeune surÂ 10 nâest ni en emploi ni en formation.

Les politiques europÃ©ennes âÂ câest ce Ã  quoi nous nous engageonsÂ â visent Ã  donner Ã  ces jeunes accÃ¨s Ã  une formation et Ã  un emploi, mais Ã  un emploi de qualitÃ©, Ã  un emploi qui leur permette de se construire un avenir et de participer non seulement Ã  la prospÃ©ritÃ©, mais surtout au bien-Ãªtre global de notre sociÃ©tÃ©.

Il ne suffira pas de fixer des lignes directrices. Cette nouvelle lÃ©gislature doit aussi servir Ã  crÃ©er des initiatives lÃ©gislatives avec un cadre clair pour la qualitÃ© de lâemploi, la prise en compte des risques psychosociaux et du surmenage, la garantie de salaires suffisants, un vÃ©ritable droit Ã  la formation,Â un cadre sur lâintelligence artificielle sur le lieu de travail et un cadre pour une juste transition. Et tout cela, dans le respect du dialogue social. VoilÃ  ce que plus de 200Â millions de travailleurs et de travailleuses attendent de nous.

Â 

Â Â MÃ©lanie Disdier (PfE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, chers collÃ¨gues, quâobtient-on quand une organisation nÃ©olibÃ©rale sans colonne vertÃ©brale, telle que la Commission europÃ©enne, cherche Ã  se mÃªler des politiques sociales de ses Ãtats membres? Ãtonnamment, nous nâobtenons pas de proposition programmÃ©e pour protÃ©ger les gens, mais plutÃ´t une politique antisociale.

Dâun cÃ´tÃ©, affublÃ©e de sa novlangue orwellienne, elle parlera de Â«vieillesse activeÂ» pour Ã©viter dâassumer sa volontÃ© de nous faire travailler toujours plus longtemps. De lâautre, sous couvert de pÃ©nurie de travailleurs, elle promeut toujours plus dâimmigration, cherchant Ã  ouvrir les vannes au profit dâune main-dâÅuvre Ã©trangÃ¨re et corvÃ©able Ã  merci.

Ce projet politique est intolÃ©rable, et il pÃ©nalise toujours les mÃªmes. Il se construit contre les nations et est profondÃ©ment antieuropÃ©en. Nous nous devons de nous y opposer, pour prÃ©server nos peuples et nos retraitÃ©s.

(L'oratrice refuse une question carton bleu de Anthony Smith)

Â Â Estrella GalÃ¡n (The Left). â SeÃ±or presidente, cuando hablamos de polÃ­ticas de empleo, lo que estamos diciendo es que hay que mejorar la vida de la gente.

Â¿Saben cÃ³mo ha logrado EspaÃ±a alcanzar nÃºmeros histÃ³ricos de empleo? Mejorando las condiciones laborales, reduciendo la tasa de temporalidad y con una reforma laboral que garantiza puestos de trabajo mÃ¡s estables, lejos de la incertidumbre que generan los contratos precarios y temporales. Una reforma que ha puesto en el centro a las personas trabajadoras. Una reforma que, por primera vez, da derechos en vez de reducirlos.

En el lado opuesto tenemos la propuesta de la nueva ComisiÃ³n, que elimina cualquier referencia a las personas trabajadoras y al trabajo bajo el eufemismo de la productividad. SeÃ±ora Von der Leyen, el mundo no se mueve por las skills, se mueve por las trabajadoras y los trabajadores. El trabajo decente y los derechos laborales deben ser un eje central en el proyecto europeo.

Y hay que seguir avanzando.Â Debemos reducir la jornada laboral para ganar tiempo y calidad de vida, porque cuando mejoramos la vida de la gente, cuando ganamos en derechos, ganamos en democracia.

Â Â Jan-Peter Warnke (NI). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, meine sehr verehrten Damen und Herren! Arbeitsbedingungen fÃ¼r EuropÃ¤er innerhalb des Binnenmarktes zu verbessern, ist eine wichtige Aufgabe dieses Hauses. Auch das vorliegende Ergebnis der EMPL-TÃ¤tigkeit kann ich begrÃ¼Ãen, und ich mÃ¶chte mich bei Li Andersson ganz besonders fÃ¼r die kollegiale und konstruktive Zusammenarbeit bedanken.

Im Gegensatz zu den EntwÃ¼rfen der Kommission weist der Text vom EMPL-Ausschuss viel Realismus und einen tiefen sozialen Grundgedanken auf; ich verweise hier auf die ÃnderungsantrÃ¤geÂ 20 und 26. Eine freie Arbeitsplatzsuche im europÃ¤ischen Binnenmarkt mit erhaltenen sozialen Grundrechten ist ein hohes Gut der EU. Aber gilt das auch fÃ¼r BeschÃ¤ftigungsfelder in der kritischen Infrastruktur unserer Gesellschaften mit permanentem Personalmangel wie den Gesundheitssystemen?

Was ist, wenn labour mobility zu brain drain fÃ¼r einzelne Nationen wird? Konkret bemÃ¤ngele ich hier die Tatsache, dass Ãrzte und Pflegepersonal, in Deutschlands NachbarlÃ¤ndern ausgebildet, nach Deutschland wechseln, weil die GehÃ¤lter vier- bis fÃ¼nfmal so hoch sind. Gerecht wÃ¤re eine Arbeitsmigration in beide Richtungen, aber das grundsÃ¤tzliche Abwandern teurer ausgebildeter FachkrÃ¤fte im Gesundheitssektor ist keine gelebte europÃ¤ische SolidaritÃ¤t.

Es existiert bereits ein Kodex der WHO, der Regeln fÃ¼r die Rekrutierung von GesundheitsfachkrÃ¤ften aus dem Ausland beschreibt. Mein Vorschlag wÃ¤re es, diesen Kodex an die europÃ¤ischen VerhÃ¤ltnisse zu adaptieren und fÃ¼r die EU in Kraft zu setzen.

Â Â SÃ©rgio Humberto (PPE). â Senhor Presidente, Caros Colegas, I will speak in Portuguese, a quarta lÃ­ngua mais falada no mundo como lÃ­ngua materna.

O que Ã© que podemos fazer para combater o desemprego, para reter os nossos talentos, para sermos atrativos para os jovens, para melhorar as condiÃ§Ãµes de emprego e proporcionar, finalmente, a toda a populaÃ§Ã£o ativa as oportunidades que merece?Â Precisamos de investir na reindustrializaÃ§Ã£o, diminuir a burocracia, ter uma imigraÃ§Ã£o regulada, qualificada e integrada e apostar, entre muitas outras coisas, tambÃ©m no nosso capital humano.

Caras e Caros Amigos, nÃ£o tenhamos medo de inovar, nÃ£o tenhamos medo de abraÃ§ar a mudanÃ§a, de nos adaptarmos Ã s necessidades do mercado e de crescer. Todos juntos somos muito mais do que 27 e, se trabalharmos todos juntos, ninguÃ©m ficarÃ¡ para trÃ¡s.

Â Â Idoia Mendia (S&D). â SeÃ±or presidente, querido comisario, primero querÃ­a empezar agradeciendo el trabajo a los ponentes. El empleo estÃ¡ en el corazÃ³n de las transformaciones verde, digital y demogrÃ¡fica, conectado a la competitividad, sÃ­, pero con un compromiso social para seguir construyendo una Europa mÃ¡s justa e inclusiva.

Defendemos el empleo digno, de calidad e inclusivo con el foco puesto en los colectivos infrarrepresentados en el mundo del empleo, como son las mujeres y los jÃ³venes: debemos garantizar su plena incorporaciÃ³n al mundo del empleo, como estamos haciendo en EspaÃ±a, donde tenemos mÃ¡s de diez millones de mujeres trabajando âmÃ¡s que nuncaâ y estamos reduciendo el desempleo de los jÃ³venes. Y, sobre todo, tampoco debemos olvidar el derecho al acceso al empleo ordinario de las personas con discapacidad.

Necesitamos todo el talento para afrontar el futuro en Europa y debemos impulsar el papel de los servicios pÃºblicos de empleo para lograr estos objetivos, para garantizar el derecho a la formaciÃ³n de toda la ciudadanÃ­a: mÃ¡s digitalizaciÃ³n y mÃ¡s integraciÃ³n europea de los servicios pÃºblicos de empleo.

Y defendemos la protecciÃ³n de los derechos de los trabajadores en materia de salarios y condiciones laborales, con el foco puesto, sobre todo, en la salud laboral y en los sistemas de protecciÃ³n social. Exigimos que se cumplan las directivas sobre salarios mÃ­nimos, transparencia salarial y plataformas digitales. Europa tiene que estar del lado de sus ciudadanos e invertir en una sociedad mÃ¡s justa e igualitaria.

Â Â Juan Carlos Girauta Vidal (PfE). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±orÃ­as, siento decirles que las orientaciones presentadas siguen teniendo un tufillo ideolÃ³gico del que parece que la UniÃ³n Europea es incapaz de liberar, o de librar, a ninguna de sus propuestas.

Mi paÃ­s, EspaÃ±a, sufre una de las mayores tasas de desempleo y la mayor de paro juvenil de la UniÃ³n. Ese tufillo ideolÃ³gico es un lujo que no podemos permitirnos; lo que se necesita son propuestas estructurales con vistas a la creaciÃ³n de empleo de calidad, para lo cual es necesario contar con un entorno adecuado.

Algunas de estas propuestas son: reducciÃ³n de impuestos para las empresas âespecialmente para las pymesâ y de las cuotas de autÃ³nomos, un modelo real de conciliaciÃ³n familiar âespecialmente para las mujeres que quieren ser madres y trabajadorasâ, el fomento de la contrataciÃ³n indefinida de nacionales frente a inmigrantes ilegales que tiran de los sueldos hacia abajo, la retirada de subvenciones a unos sindicatos que ya son meros tÃ­teres del poder polÃ­tico sin representatividad efectiva...

En definitiva, frente a propuestas contaminadas de ideologÃ­a, proponemos la aplicaciÃ³n de polÃ­ticas alternativas de sentido comÃºn.

Â Â Francesco Torselli (ECR). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, come membro della commissione per l'occupazione e gli affari sociali, vorrei condividere oggi con voi il piacere e l'orgoglio nell'intervenire â da italiano â su un tema come quello delle politiche in favore dell'occupazione e di potervi presentare i risultati raggiunti dall'Italia negli ultimi due anni.

Noi oggi chiediamo all'Unione europea di favorire l'occupazione. In Italia negli ultimi due anni sono aumentati di 834.000 i posti di lavoro e gli occupati hanno raggiunto la cifra record di 24 milioni.

Chiediamo all'Unione europea di promuovere l'istruzione STEM, per favorire l'inserimento dei giovani nel mercato del lavoro. Ebbene, in Italia negli ultimi due anni abbiamo investito 950 milioni nell'istruzione STEM e 545 per contrastare la dispersione scolastica.

Colleghi, oggi l'Italia di Giorgia Meloni puÃ² guardarvi negli occhi a testa alta e offrire a tutti voi una strada per arrivare a contrastare la disoccupazione e a favorire il rilancio del mercato del lavoro.

Â Â Catarina Martins (The Left). â Senhor Presidente, como explicar salÃ¡rios diferentes entre trabalhadores lado a lado, mesmas funÃ§Ãµes, o mesmo posto de trabalho, responsabilidades e qualificaÃ§Ã£o? Chamaâse outsourcing, subcontrataÃ§Ã£o ou outro esquema para baixar salÃ¡rios, proteger patrÃµes e colocar trabalhadores contra trabalhadores. Chamaâse abuso de quem estÃ¡ mais desprotegido porque Ã© subcontratado, indocumentado, mulher ou minoria, para baixar os salÃ¡rios de toda a gente.

O esquema Ã© velho: concorrÃªncia direta entre trabalhadores e entre paÃ­ses, numa corrida dos salÃ¡rios para o fundo, nos setores tradicionais, como na economia digital. A ComissÃ£o Europeia tambÃ©m podia chamarâlhe concorrÃªncia desleal, mas nÃ£o chama. Diz coisas bonitas sobre o emprego e, depois, impÃµe as piores prÃ¡ticas econÃ³micas e laborais.

Cumprimento a relatora Li Andersson por lembrar isso mesmo, ao exigir contrataÃ§Ã£o coletiva, responsabilidade social e transiÃ§Ã£o justa. Saibam as instituiÃ§Ãµes europeias e os governos responder por isso mesmo.

Â Â Henrik Dahl (PPE). â Hr. formand! Tak for ordet. Det forslag vi diskuterer i dag, er super sympatisk. Det er styret af gode hensigter, men som altid er det sÃ¥dan, at djÃ¦vlen gemmer sig i detaljen. Den detalje, jeg som dansker finder sÃ¦rligt djÃ¦velsk, er, at medlemsstaterne hurtigst muligt skal implementere mindstelÃ¸nsdirektivet. Danmark har for fÃ¸rste gang i historien indledt en retssag mod EU med det formÃ¥l at fjerne mindstelÃ¸nsdirektivet - den har Sverige tilsluttet sig. I de to lande mener vi nemlig ikke, at forslaget er inden for rammerne af traktaten. Den fastslÃ¥r nemlig, at borgernes lÃ¸nforhold ligger uden for EU's kompetence. Alligevel blev mindstelÃ¸nsdirektivet vedtaget for Ã¥r tilbage, og nu stÃ¥r vi her i dag. Med et nyt Parlament og snart en ny Kommission er tiden sÃ¥ ikke kommet til, at vi begynder at tage subsidiaritetsprincippet alvorligt? Vores system er ikke i stykker. Der er ikke nogen grund til at reparere det.

Â Â Romana JerkoviÄ (S&D). â PoÅ¡tovani predsjedavajuÄi, europsko trÅ¾iÅ¡te rada suoÄava se danas s brojnim izazovima. Nedostatak radne snage, nedostatak vjeÅ¡tina, zelena i digitalna tranzicija, sve to ukazuje na vaÅ¾nost inovativnog, konkurentnog i prilagodljivog trÅ¾iÅ¡ta.

MeÄutim, u stvaranju konkurentnog trÅ¾iÅ¡ta ne smijemo zaboraviti na socijalne vrijednosti. Ne smijemo Å¾rtvovati temeljna radniÄka prava. Mi danas trebamo snaÅ¾ne sindikate koji Äe pomoÄi i Å¡tititi ta prava, prava poput prava na sigurne i dostojanstvene uvjete rada i prava na plaÄu od koje se moÅ¾e dostojanstveno Å¾ivjeti.

Ove aÅ¾urirane smjernice za politike zapoÅ¡ljavanja drÅ¾ava Älanica o kojima danas govorimo potiÄu drÅ¾ave na stvaranje kvalitetnih radnih mjesta, uz rjeÅ¡avanje manjka radne snage u kljuÄnim sektorima poput zdravstva, obrazovanja i STEM podruÄja. Fokus je i na cjeloÅ¾ivotnom uÄenju i obrazovanju koje Äe radnike uÄiniti konkurentnijima na trÅ¾iÅ¡tu rada.Â Ove smjernice ukazuju i na vaÅ¾nost zaÅ¡tite najranjivijih skupina na trÅ¾iÅ¡tu rada, kao Å¡to su mladi, Å¾ene i osobe s invaliditetom.

To ne smiju ostati samo lijepe rijeÄi na papiru. VaÅ¾no je da stvorimo jednake moguÄnosti za sve radnike s adekvatnom i odrÅ¾ivom socijalnom zaÅ¡titom.

Da, mi trebamo Europsku uniju koja Äe biti ekonomski stabilna i jaka te konkurentna, ali mora biti i socijalno pravedna. To je jedini ispravni put.

Â Â Kathleen Funchion (The Left). â A UachtarÃ¡in, firstly, I wish to commend Li Andersson for this progressive report. I welcome the emphasis on education, training, better pay and conditions. And I also note the key message that affordable housing is key to addressing social inequalities. A decent home is fundamental for every worker to thrive and prosper. In Ireland, workers are burdened with sky-high house prices and also rip-off rents. As we speak today, 14Â 000 people are homeless, which includes 4Â 000 children.

For too long, the government in Ireland and, indeed, European institutions have been putting vested and financial interests ahead of workers and families. As legislators, we must act. We must repeal the Non-Performing Loans Directive, which is a directive for vulture funds, and we need to amend state aid rules and an exemption in the fiscal rules to enable us to invest in housing, which every single citizen of Europe should have as a right. We need to ensure workers are supported.

Â Â Isabelle Le Callennec (PPE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, malgrÃ© ses plus de 13Â millions de chÃ´meurs, lâEurope est confrontÃ©e non pas Ã  une pÃ©nurie dâemplois, mais Ã  une pÃ©nurie de main-dâÅuvre. Le taux de chÃ´mage moyen y est de 5,9Â %. Onze pays font moins bien, dont la France, avec 7,5Â %.

Ces lignes directrices invitent les Ãtats membres Ã  accorder aux personnes sans emploi des prestations chÃ´mage appropriÃ©es pendant une durÃ©e raisonnable, en fonction des cotisations versÃ©es et des rÃ¨gles nationales, dans le respect du principe de subsidiaritÃ©. Le texte prÃ©cise: Â«Ces prestations ne devraient pas dÃ©courager un retour rapide Ã  lâemploi et devraient sâaccompagner [â¦] de mesures de perfectionnement et de reconversion professionnelsÂ». Câest prÃ©cisÃ©ment lâune des vocations du Fonds social europÃ©en, dotÃ© de prÃ¨s de 143Â milliardsÂ dâeuros pour la pÃ©riodeÂ 2021-2027.Â Il convient donc de mesurer la contribution du FSE Ã  ce retour rapide Ã  lâemploi.

Chers collÃ¨gues, Monsieur le Commissaire, les transitions numÃ©rique et Ã©cologique se rÃ©percutent fortement sur le marchÃ© de lâemploi. Les outils dâaccompagnement des potentielles victimes collatÃ©rales se doivent dâÃªtre efficients et simples dâaccÃ¨s.

Â Â

Â Â Marit Maij (S&D). â Voorzitter, commissaris, als we het hebben over onze arbeidsmarkt en over werkgelegenheid, dan gaat het opvallend vaak alleen over concurrentievermogen, vaardigheden en ook over hoe bedrijven met zo min mogelijk mensen in dienst zoveel mogelijk winst kunnen maken. Van die winst zou iedereen moeten profiteren. Toch blijkt dat vooral bedrijven, werkgevers en hun aandeelhouders ervan profiteren.

We moeten af van dit frame. Het frame dat werk er alleen maar is om te zorgen voor winst. Want werk, dat is ook zingeving voor mensen en een manier om een boterham te verdienen. Werk is ook een manier om in een sociale omgeving terecht te komen en dat moeten we beschermen met goede lonen, goede arbeidsvoorwaarden en goede ontwikkelingsmogelijkheden.

Ik hoop dat dit ook een prioriteit zal zijn bij de Europese Commissie, de lidstaten en de bedrijven zelf. Want werk is echt heel veel meer dan alleen maar een winstmachine.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Angelika Winzig (PPE). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Herr Kommissar, Kolleginnen und Kollegen! WÃ¤hrend die USA in den letzten Jahren mit gÃ¼nstiger Energie, wenig BÃ¼rokratie und mit dem Inflation Reduction Act ihre WettbewerbsfÃ¤higkeit steigern konnten, hinkt Europa nach. Timmermans GrÃ¼ner Deal hatte ursprÃ¼nglich auch einen Deal fÃ¼r die Wirtschaft versprochen; was kam aber, waren Belastungen fÃ¼r Landwirtschaft und Wirtschaft, und das Wirtschaftswachstum stagniert.

Europa war nie reich an Rohstoffen, aber reich an klugen KÃ¶pfen, die allerdings aufgrund der demografischen Entwicklung auch leider weniger werden. Daher braucht es umfassende MaÃnahmen, denn gut ausgebildete FachkrÃ¤fte sind der SchlÃ¼ssel, um im globalen Wettbewerb bestehen zu kÃ¶nnen.

In Ãsterreich, aber auch in der Schweiz und in Deutschland hat sich die duale Ausbildung, die praktische Berufserfahrung mit theoretischem Wissen verbindet, als Erfolgsmodell erwiesen. Das kÃ¶nnte auch als Vorbild fÃ¼r Europa dienen, denn Bildung ist der Garant gegen Arbeitslosigkeit und fÃ¼r Wohlstand in der EU.

Â Â Niels Geuking (PPE). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Herr Kommissar Schmit! Gestern nahm ich an der Debatte zur StÃ¤rkung des Binnenmarktes zur Schaffung einer nachhaltigen Zukunft und von Wohlstand fÃ¼r alle BÃ¼rgerinnen und BÃ¼rger in der EU teil. Und eines wurde unter anderem klar deutlich: Wir brauchen auch sozialÃ¶konomische Standards innerhalb der EuropÃ¤ischen Union, um einerseits Wettbewerbsverzerrungen zu verhindern und andererseits den erarbeiteten Wohlstand auch vielschichtiger zu ermÃ¶glichen.

Die Leitlinien haben das Potenzial, das Leben von Millionen Menschen positiv zu beeinflussen. Die Mitgliedstaaten sind jetzt in der Pflicht. Der Schwerpunkt liegt diesmal insbesondere auf allgemeiner und beruflicher BildungÂ â bei der hohen Jugendarbeitslosigkeit in Europa auch ein SchlÃ¼ssel, um dem FachkrÃ¤ftemangel effektiv zu begegnen. Der Fokus liegt zudem auf den neuen Technologien: kÃ¼nstliche Intelligenz, bezahlbarer Wohnraum usw.

So sind die Leitlinien ein wichtiger Beitrag zur Umsetzung der europÃ¤ischen SÃ¤ule sozialer Rechte und ein weiterer Schritt zu einem sozial zusammenwachsenden Europa. Wir mÃ¼ssen dranbleiben und gerade fÃ¼r unsere Kinder und Familien mehr machen.

Â Â SunÄana Glavak (PPE). â PoÅ¡tovani predsjedavajuÄi, svima je jasno da se politika za promicanje zapoÅ¡ljavanja i socijalne zaÅ¡tite Europske unije suoÄava s nizom izazova koji zahtijevaju daljnje prilagodbe i reforme.

Digitalizacija, zelena tranzicija, borba protiv nezaposlenosti mladih i nesigurnih oblika rada, kao i borba protiv socijalnih nejednakosti, to su problemi koje bismo morali rijeÅ¡iti kako bismo osigurali ono Å¡to Å¾elimo, a to je prije svega odrÅ¾ivi rast, socijalna kohezija i visokokvalitetna radna mjesta, a ovdje smo Äuli danas i priuÅ¡tivo stanovanje za mlade, koje Äini veliki problem u brojnim Älanicama Europske unije.

Ja dolazim iz Hrvatske koja ima najmanju nezaposlenost i najveÄu zaposlenost u posljednjih desetak godina. Stvoreno je 308 000 radnih mjesta. ProsjeÄna neto plaÄa porasla je za 76 posto. Kreditni rejting moje zemlje je a minus, s pozitivnim izgledima. Preduvjet za to bila je, naravno, politiÄka stabilnost, koja je omoguÄila i kontinuitet politika, i to ispravnih politika.

MeÄutim, postoji znaÄajna razlika u stopama zaposlenosti i socijalnoj zaÅ¡titi meÄu drÅ¾avama Älanicama. SuoÄavaju se s veÄim izazovima, ukljuÄujuÄi visoku nezaposlenost, slabije socijalne mreÅ¾e.

Kolegice i kolege, 2017. godine usvojili smo dokument Europski stup socijalnih prava, izmeÄu ostalog 20 naÄela, a jedno od njih je jednake moguÄnosti i pristup trÅ¾iÅ¡tu rada. Ono Å¡to mi sada apostrofiramo da to nismo apsolvirali i da je pred nama joÅ¡ puno posla, a pred nama su i zaposlenja moguÄnosti s kojima se joÅ¡ nismo susreli.

ZakljuÄit Äu, kako bi se osigurala dugoroÄna stabilnost, potrebne su dodatne mjere i rjeÅ¡avanje strukturnih problema, kao i demografskih izazova koji su pred nama.

Interventions Ã  la demande

Â

Â Â JoÃ£o Oliveira (The Left). â Senhor Presidente, a posiÃ§Ã£o do Parlamento sobre as orientaÃ§Ãµes das polÃ­ticas de emprego melhora a proposta da ComissÃ£o ao Conselho, mas nÃ£o apaga o enquadramento destas orientaÃ§Ãµes polÃ­ticas, nem os seus prejuÃ­zos para os trabalhadores. Apesar de curtas e insuficientes, consideramos positivas as referÃªncias aos salÃ¡rios justos, a condiÃ§Ãµes de trabalho dignas, Ã  contrataÃ§Ã£o coletiva, aos objetivos do pleno emprego e da erradicaÃ§Ã£o da pobreza, Ã  necessidade de dar resposta Ã  crise da habitaÃ§Ã£o.

Mas, estas orientaÃ§Ãµes estÃ£o enquadradas no processo da governaÃ§Ã£o econÃ³mica e do Semestre Europeu, essa teia de imposiÃ§Ãµes, chantagens e condicionamentos Ã s polÃ­ticas soberanas dos Estados, incluindo em matÃ©ria de emprego. NÃ£o Ã© possÃ­vel apagar as consequÃªncias negativas dessas imposiÃ§Ãµes para os trabalhadores, no aumento do desemprego, na degradaÃ§Ã£o das condiÃ§Ãµes sociais e laborais, na desregulamentaÃ§Ã£o das relaÃ§Ãµes de trabalho, na contenÃ§Ã£o dos salÃ¡rios, reformas e pensÃµes.

Queremos uma polÃ­tica de emprego e de aprofundamento dos direitos laborais e sociais, livre do Semestre Europeu e de outros mecanismos de constrangimentos e condicionamentos.

Â Â

Â Â Lukas Sieper (NI). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, liebe Mitmenschen! Die Arbeitspolitik ist aus einem ganz entscheidenden Punkt ein wesentlicher Bestandteil jeder politischen Entscheidung, die wir hier in der EU treffen; schlieÃlich ist die eigene Arbeit den Menschen, die wir vertreten, am nÃ¤chsten.

Wenn wir wirklich wollen, dass sie zu Europa stehen, dass sie zu dieser Idee eines gemeinsamen Kontinents stehen, eines gemeinsamen Raums von Frieden, Freiheit und Recht, dann brauchen sie dafÃ¼r eine vernÃ¼nftige Grundlage, und diese Grundlage wollen sie sich selber schaffen. Deswegen sind, wie einige Kollegen hier zu Recht gesagt haben, die Leitlinien ein wichtiger Beitrag zur Umsetzung der europÃ¤ischen Idee.

Wir sollten uns immer darauf zurÃ¼ckbesinnen, dass diese Menschen, die wir vertreten, vor allen Dingen Menschen sind, die einen ganz normalen Job haben; keine Menschen, die in einem groÃen, halbrunden Saal sitzen und Ã¼ber wichtige Dinge debattieren.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(Fin des interventions Ã  la demande)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Nicolas Schmit, Member of the Commission. â Mr President, first, I would like to thank you for this rich debate. I am confident that your report will be successfully adopted tomorrow. Taking your important work into account, the employment guidelines will help steer employment and social policy coordination in the European Semester, following the economic governance review, alongside, and in a more integrated fashion, vis-Ã -vis fiscal and macroeconomic policies.

It is true that we have a big shortage of labour and of skills in Europe, and this is a big problem in most of our Member States. But at the same time, and it has been mentioned, we should not forget that we still have more than 13Â million people out of a job. Unemployed people. Young people, elderly workers, vulnerable categories have all the difficulties to find a job. So I think there is a need still for strong employment policies and especially very strong skills and reskilling policies.

Only with our joint efforts and commitments can we ensure that our European social market economy continues to be successful in Europe and on a global scale. It's true, yes, Europe needs to be even more competitive. But competitiveness is not an aim in itself. The real objective should be quality jobs, which means decent wages, good working conditions with social protection and the opportunity for lifelong learning.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Li Andersson, rapporteur. â Mr President, I want to thank all colleagues for this wide-ranging discussion and debate, and also for the broad support that was voiced for this report. Some colleagues pointed out that you think these things should be dealt with on a national level, but as one colleague said, rightly, this is not a legally binding instrument. So if you think that, on a national level, Member States should focus on decent working conditions, on ensuring a balanced labour market, securing jobs for everyone, then you should absolutely vote in favour of a recommendation to the Member States to do exactly this.

I think these guidelines are also an important policy document, a statement from the Parliament regarding how we want to ensure fair and balanced employment policies in Europe, how we think Member States should address issues facing working life, and all of the millions of Europeans who have not yet found their own place and their own role in the labour market. As has been pointed out, this legislative term will be much about competitiveness, but it's worth stating that there can be no competitiveness in Europe without worker well-being, without making sure that not a single European worker will get sick from his or her working environment. We need to make sure that we secure decent working conditions, education and training, and that we also put social dialogue and collective bargaining at the heart of how we define European competitiveness.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Le PrÃ©sident. â Le dÃ©bat est clos.

Le vote aura lieu demain.

Kitas klausimas â tai veiksmai, kuriÅ³ turi imtis SÄjunga ir valstybÄs narÄs, siekdamos uÅ¾kirsti keliÄ neigiamoms socialinÄms nedarbo ir skurdo pasekmÄms visuomenÄje. Â Â Le PrÃ©sident. â Je voudrais faire une annonce en application de lâarticleÂ 251, paragrapheÂ 4, du rÃ¨glement intÃ©rieur, pour vous informer quâil nây a pas eu de demande de mise aux voix des neuf rectificatifs annoncÃ©s en sÃ©ance plÃ©niÃ¨re hier, Ã  lâouverture de la sÃ©ance. Â 

20. Situation in Azerbaijan, violation of human rights and international law and relations with Armenia (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â Le PrÃ©sident. â Lâordre du jour appelle le dÃ©bat sur la dÃ©claration de la Commission sur la situation en AzerbaÃ¯djan, la violation des droits de lâhomme et du droit international, et les relations avec lâArmÃ©nie [2024/2890(RSP)].

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Nicolas Schmit, Member of the Commission. â MrÂ President, Honourable Members, today's debate is taking place less than three weeks before the beginning of the COP29 UN Climate Change Conference in Baku. The conference will gather world leaders and the international climate community to advance on the global climate agenda. But it will also draw the world's attention to the conference's host country.

Our debate today is therefore very timely. COP29 is an opportunity for the Azerbaijani authorities to demonstrate a commitment to their international human rights obligations. It is an opportunity to reverse the worrying trend of the past year of an increasingly shrinking space for civil society, intensifying repression of independent media and dissenting voices, and the growing number of arbitrary arrests.

This House has drawn attention to a number of particularly unsettling cases, including the fate of Gubad Ibadoghlu, Ilhamiz Guliyev, Bakhtiyar Hajiyev, Alasgar Mammadli, and Anar Mammadli. There are more, many more, including, since this summer, Bahruz Samadov, a young scholar and peace activist studying in Prague who was arrested while visiting family in Azerbaijan.

Our position is clear: the Government of Azerbaijan should release all those who have been detained for exercising their fundamental rights, including journalists, human rights defenders and political activists. Torture and ill treatment are absolutely prohibited under international law, and any reports of abuses should be promptly and impartially investigated. The authorities must ensure due process, dignified treatment of all detainees and access to proper healthcare in accordance with international standards.

We, in particular, urge the government to lift DrÂ Ibadoghlu's travel ban without delay to allow him to access the medical treatment he needs abroad. We also hope that the Azerbaijani Government reconsiders the travel ban imposed on 76 PACE members, merely for how they voted in the Council of Europe Parliamentary Assembly.

Azerbaijan's participation in the Council of Europe is in the fundamental interest of all Azerbaijanis and all of Europe. COP29 offers the international community an opportunity to reiterate these messages. And during these two recent visits to Baku, Commissioner Hoekstra raised the human rights situation with the authorities and met with civil society representatives, including 2024 Sakharov Prize finalist DrÂ Ibadoghlu.

COP29 is also an historic opportunity for peace. Azerbaijan and Armenia have never been so close to overcoming decades of conflict. The conference will be a historic chance for the Azerbaijan and Armenia to demonstrate to the world their commitment to peace and to muster the political will to sign a peace agreement. As European Union, we continue to lend our full support to the normalisation process, and we stand ready to mobilise the necessary resources to allow all sides to reap the benefits of a lasting and sustainable peace.

Azerbaijan faces important choices both as regards the human rights situation in the country and the peace process with its neighbour. We should keep our channels of communication open and use our relationship to promote human rights, peace and stability in Azerbaijan and in the whole region.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â FranÃ§ois-Xavier Bellamy, au nom du groupe PPE. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, dans trois semaines, la COPÂ 29 commencera Ã  Bakou. Chez IlhamÂ Aliyev. Aliyev, bourreau du peuple armÃ©nien, quâil a attaquÃ© Ã  plusieurs reprises pour le chasser des terres quâil occupait historiquement dans le Haut-Karabagh, et quâil attaque aussi dÃ©sormais sur son territoire mÃªme, la RÃ©publique souveraine dâArmÃ©nie. Aliyev, qui sâattaque Ã  nos pays, qui sâattaque Ã  toute lâEurope en dÃ©stabilisant la France: en Nouvelle-CalÃ©donie, comme dans de nombreux autres dÃ©partements et territoires dâoutre-mer, et jusque dans nos mÃ©dias.

Aliyev, qui, sur notre sol, vient pourchasser ses opposants politiques. Je pense Ã  MahammadÂ Mirzali, victime de plusieurs tentatives dâassassinat sur le sol franÃ§ais. Je pense Ã  Toural Sadigli. Je pense Ã©videmment Ã  Vidadi Isgandarli, qui a Ã©tÃ© enterrÃ© hier aprÃ¨s avoir Ã©tÃ© assassinÃ© Ã  Mulhouse. Mahammad, Toural et le frÃ¨re de Vidadi sont avec nous aujourdâhui dans cet hÃ©micycle. Nous voulons leur dire que nous sommes Ã  leurs cÃ´tÃ©s dans ce combat pour la libertÃ© du peuple azerbaÃ¯djanais.

MonsieurÂ Aliyev, si vous croyez tant Ã  votre propre pouvoir, pourquoi tenter de faire taire les voix qui vous contestent? Si vous croyez tant Ã  votre lÃ©gitimitÃ©, pourquoi user de la violence? Il est temps, chers amis, que lâEurope sorte enfin de son inconscience. Monsieur le Commissaire, merci pour les mots forts que vous avez eus Ã  lâinstant. Enfin! Mais non, la COPÂ 29 nâest pas une chance. La COPÂ 29 est un scandale, une honte qui salira lâhistoire mÃªme de lâengagement pour la protection de lâenvironnement. Ce gaz que nous achetons Ã  ce pays tÃ©moigne, lÃ  encore, de notre lÃ¢chetÃ© collective.

Il est temps de dÃ©noncer aujourdâhui lâaccord gazier qui lie la Commission europÃ©enne Ã  lâAzerbaÃ¯djan, de prendre les sanctions qui sâimposent contre Ilham Aliyev et son rÃ©gime, et de dire enfin que la dÃ©mocratie et le cÅur battant de lâEurope sont du cÃ´tÃ© du peuple armÃ©nien, qui se bat pour la libertÃ©, pour la paix, pour la sÃ©curitÃ©, dans cette rÃ©gion du Caucase comme dans le monde entier.

Â

Â Â Jordan Bardella, au nom du groupe PfE. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident. Monsieur le Commissaire, mes chers collÃ¨gues, aprÃ¨s lâeffondrement de la RÃ©publique du Haut-Karabakh, survenu Ã  la suite des opÃ©rations militaires menÃ©es par lâAzerbaÃ¯djan en septembreÂ 2023, lâArmÃ©nie est aujourdâhui la cible des vellÃ©itÃ©s expansionnistes du rÃ©gime dâAliyev. Le rÃ©gime azerbaÃ¯djanais, enrichi par lâargent que lui verse lâEurope pour lâachat de son gaz et aidÃ© par le sultan ErdoÄan, intensifie ses actions militaires pour maintenir lâArmÃ©nie dans une position de faiblesse et dâisolement dans le Caucase.

Depuis 2023, une campagne de dÃ©sinformation orchestrÃ©e par le rÃ©gime de Bakou, documentÃ©e par la presse et dÃ©noncÃ©e par le ministÃ¨re de lâintÃ©rieur franÃ§ais, vise des territoires dâoutre-mer de notre pays, notamment la Nouvelle-CalÃ©donie et la Martinique. Nous devons condamner ici ces pratiques avec la plus grande force et avec la plus grande fermetÃ©. LâAzerbaÃ¯djan cherche Ã  attiser, auprÃ¨s dâune frange minoritaire mais radicalisÃ©e, un sentiment antifranÃ§ais indÃ©pendantiste appelant Ã  la violence contre les biens et les personnes reprÃ©sentant la France par-delÃ  les mers.

Cette stratÃ©gie de dÃ©stabilisation outranciÃ¨re de la part dâune puissance Ã©trangÃ¨re dans nos territoires dâoutre-mer constitue une atteinte grave Ã  la souverainetÃ© franÃ§aise et Ã  la paix civile. Ces opÃ©rations que mÃ¨ne lâAzerbaÃ¯djan sont bien Ã©videmment la rÃ©ponse au soutien franÃ§ais Ã  lâArmÃ©nie, plus vieux pays chrÃ©tien de lâhistoire et vÃ©ritable pont entre lâOrient et lâOccident.

Cette rÃ©alitÃ© ne peut plus Ãªtre dÃ©libÃ©rÃ©ment occultÃ©e, par lÃ¢chetÃ© ou par complaisance vis-Ã -vis des ambitions dâexpansion portÃ©es par ErdoÄan et par ses alliÃ©s aux portes de lâEurope. Si rien ne change, alors les lÃ¢chetÃ©s dâaujourdâhui seront les chaÃ®nes de demain. Le sang des ArmÃ©niens vaut plus que le gaz dâAliyev.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Åerban Dimitrie Sturdza, Ã®n numele grupului ECR. â Domnule preÈedinte, stimaÈi colegi, condamn cu fermitate deficienÈele Èi derapajele grave cu care se confruntÄ azi Azerbaidjanul Ã®n domeniul libertÄÈilor fundamentale Èi al respectÄrii minoritÄÈilor de pe teritoriul sÄu. Este trist sÄ vedem cum acest stat se Ã®ndepÄrteazÄ tot mai mult de normele democratice Èi de standardele internaÈionale din domeniul respectÄrii drepturilor omului.

DeÈi dorim sÄ privilegiem relaÈiile comerciale Èi energetice cu Azerbaidjanul, este clar cÄ trebuie sÄ fim mult mai vigilenÈi cu privire la provenienÈa gazului care este livrat Europei. Nu putem sÄ acceptÄm gaz rusesc Ã®n Europa venind pe alte cÄi. Ãn acelaÈi timp, trebuie sÄ fim atenÈi sÄ nu periclitÄm securitatea energeticÄ a statelor membre ale Uniunii Europene.

Doresc sÄ-mi exprim profunda solidaritate cu poporul armean, creÈtin ortodox Èi Ã®ndeosebi cu cei peste 120 000 de armeni care au fost forÈaÈi sÄ Ã®Èi pÄrÄseascÄ locuinÈele din regiunea Nagorno-Karabah din cauza agresiunilor repetate ale Azerbaidjanului.

Ãn final, solicit Uniunii Europene Èi comunitÄÈii internaÈionale sÄ se asigure cÄ Azerbaidjanul conservÄ patrimoniul cultural, istoric Èi religios al poporului armean. Acest conflict necesitÄ o soluÈie bazatÄ pe echilibru Èi respectarea drepturilor omului Ã®ntru stabilitatea regiunii Èi a vecinÄtÄÈii Uniunii Europene.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Nathalie Loiseau, au nom du groupe Renew. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, en accueillant la COPÂ 29 Ã  Bakou, lâAzerbaÃ¯djan a souhaitÃ© faire parler de lui. Eh bien, exauÃ§ons-le et parlons-en. Je voudrais remercier ceux qui, avec moi, ont prÃ©parÃ© le texte que nous soumettrons Ã  votre vote, câest-Ã -dire tous les groupes Ã  lâexception des Patriotes pour lâEurope et de lâEurope des nations souveraines, qui nâont pas pris la peine de travailler sur cette rÃ©solution.

Passons sur lâÃ©coblanchiment offert Ã  un pays qui nâexporte pratiquement que des hydrocarbures. ArrÃªtons-nous un instant sur une bizarrerie: Bakou importe desÂ hydrocarbures, depuis la Russie, et de plus en plus. Quand nous signons avec Aliyev et que nous lui achetons du gaz, nous aidons indirectement Poutine. Bel exemple dâincohÃ©rence.

Il faudrait plus dâune minute pour faire la liste complÃ¨te des violations des droits de lâhomme dont le rÃ©gime dâIlham Aliyev est coupable. Je voudrais simplement citer deux personnes, pour saluer leur courage et les protÃ©ger de la vindicte de Bakou: Goubad Ibadoghlou, dont nous avons fait Ã  juste titre un finaliste du prix Sakharov, et MahammadÂ Mirzali, rÃ©fugiÃ© en Europe, et qui a pourtant subi trois tentatives dâassassinat. Il est prÃ©sent avec nous aujourdâhui, et je voudrais le saluer.Â Nous devons tenir IlhamÂ Aliyev personnellement responsable de leur sort.

Permettez-moi dâinsister encore sur les 100Â 000Â ArmÃ©niens du Haut-Karabakh, Ã  qui lâAzerbaÃ¯djan a imposÃ© un nettoyage ethnique en rÃ¨gle, sur les prisonniers de guerre toujours dÃ©tenus, sur le patrimoine armÃ©nien dÃ©truit et sur les prÃ©textes que ne cesse dâinvoquer Bakou pour repousser la paix avec lâArmÃ©nie.

Non, lâAzerbaÃ¯djan nâa dÃ©cidÃ©ment rien dâun partenaire fiable. Et oui, il faut boycotter la COPÂ 29 Ã  Bakou.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sergey Lagodinsky, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. â MrÂ President, AnarÂ Mammadli has been a known critic of Azerbaijani authorities and a fierce advocate for human rights and environmental justice, also ahead of COP29. He has long been a target of persecution. He was subjected to smear campaigns. They were following him in the streets. In fact, this April, while he was on his way to pick up his own child, he was arrested and driven away. Ever since, he is in a pre-trial detention; his home has been searched.

He is just one of many stories of this harsh reality in Azerbaijan today. Activists and critics are being imprisoned for executing their basic freedoms. As we speak, dozens of others, journalists like SevinjÂ Vagifgyzy. Activists like BahruzÂ Samadov are also behind bars for nothing more than their courage to speak out.

This is unacceptable and it is happening just weeks before Azerbaijan is set to host the United Nations climate conference. Azerbaijan is not just a regional player. It is a key partner for the European Union in energy and security issues. The President of the Commission even called the country a reliable partner. But with such partnerships comes responsibilities and this responsibility is to respect the fundamental principles of human rights, democracy and the rule of law.

We demand the immediate release of AnarÂ Mammadli, of GubadÂ Ibadoghlu. This is not just about individual cases, it is about the future of this country and of our relationship. Azerbaijan has a choice: it can continue down the path of repression or become a democracy. We urge you to choose wisely.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Alexander Sell, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. â Herr PrÃ¤sident! Im vergangenen Jahr hat die Armee von Aserbaidschan Bergkarabach angegriffen und besetzt. Ãber 100Â 000 Armenier mussten fliehen; das kulturelle Erbe des ersten christlichen Staates der Welt wird systematisch zerstÃ¶rt. Warum erfahren wir so wenig Ã¼ber diesen Konflikt? Im Januar dieses Jahres hat die Generalstaatsanwaltschaft in MÃ¼nchen Anklage gegen Politiker von CDU und CSU erhoben. Der Vorwurf: Bestechung durch die Regierung von Aserbaidschan.

Es geht um vier Millionen Euro. Vier Millionen Euro Schmiergeld fÃ¼r die Deckung der ethnischen SÃ¤uberungen in Bergkarabach. Europaweit sollen Ã¼ber 30Â Millionen geflossen sein. Es ist deshalb kein Wunder, dass wir so wenig Ã¼ber die Christenverfolgung in Bergkarabach erfahren. Die armenischen Christen werden vertrieben, weil deutsche Christdemokraten fÃ¼rs Wegschauen bezahlt werden.

Impfdeals, Maskenskandal, Aserbaidschan-AffÃ¤re: Ob im Deutschen Bundestag oder hier im EuropÃ¤ischen ParlamentÂ â die Politik dieser CDU ist nicht wertegeleitet, sie ist kÃ¤uflich.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Rasa JukneviÄienÄ (PPE). â Mr President, dear colleagues, this year's Nobel Prize in Economics was awarded to scientists who demonstrated that societies with poor rule of law and institutions that exploit their population do not generate prosperity or positive change. The South Caucasus region is now exactly the place where this theory can be tested. While today it may appear that regimes â like the Aliyev regime â trampling on the rule of law and democracy are strong, we need to take the long view.

That is why Armenia's shift away from Russia and towards a European way of life must be a priority for the European Union. This weekend showed the toxic role the Kremlin played in Moldova's elections, and revealed the extent of its infiltration in Georgia's current government. We also clearly see how Putin is exploiting long-standing historical tensions between Azeris and Armenians.

Therefore, today we agreed among the groups on a resolution, and we are very clear: the European Parliament condemns the direction that the Aliyev regime continues to take and expresses clear support for Armenia's chosen European way of life.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Nacho SÃ¡nchez Amor (S&D). â SeÃ±or presidente, hay una tensiÃ³n tradicional entre los regÃ­menes autoritarios, que buscan ser blanqueados mediante la organizaciÃ³n de grandes eventos, y quienes piensan que estas ocasiones son una oportunidad tambiÃ©n para, precisamente, subrayar esa represiÃ³n polÃ­tica. Malas noticias: van ganando los primeros.

El evento es la excusa para una vuelta de tuerca a la represiÃ³n, justo para no enturbiar la celebraciÃ³n. Y sin consecuencias. Ese es el centro de la cuestiÃ³n: sin consecuencias. Pensemos en el Mundial de Qatar, los Juegos OlÃ­mpicos de PekÃ­n o los Juegos de Invierno de Sochi. Y sigue sin haber consecuencias, lo que es, en sÃ­ mismo, un incentivo para repetir; sobre todo, si el paÃ­s tiene gas o petrÃ³leo, porque entonces recibes amables visitas de la ComisiÃ³n, palmaditas en la espalda y te llaman Â«socio fiableÂ», mientras tu PolicÃ­a sigue aterrorizando, acosando, silenciando y encarcelando, como pasÃ³ en AzerbaiyÃ¡n en EurovisiÃ³n 2012 o en los Juegos Europeos de 2015.

Los defensores de derechos humanos ya temen que sus paÃ­ses sean elegidos para grandes eventos, porque serÃ¡ la seÃ±al para limpiar las calles, las redacciones de los medios, las redes sociales, de cualquier atisbo de crÃ­tica, pues habrÃ¡ muchos periodistas: a mÃ¡s testigos, mÃ¡s represiÃ³n.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Pierre-Romain Thionnet (PfE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, avant comme aprÃ¨s la tempÃªte, il peut rÃ©gner un calme qui vous fait baisser la garde. Dans le Caucase du Sud, Ã©talant devant eux des cartes soviÃ©tiques, autoritÃ©s armÃ©niennes et azerbaÃ¯djanaises discutent du traitÃ© de leurs frontiÃ¨res. LâArtsakh est tombÃ©, et demain câest la frontiÃ¨re mÃªme de la RÃ©publique dâArmÃ©nie qui pourrait Ãªtre visÃ©e, en particulier la rÃ©gion du Syunik, dont le contrÃ´le permettrait aux AzerbaÃ¯djanais de rÃ©aliser une continuitÃ© territoriale turque de la MÃ©diterranÃ©e Ã  la Caspienne. La protection de lâArmÃ©nie chrÃ©tienne par la Russie Ã©tait une illusion. Elle avait pour condition lâalignement dâErevan sur Moscou.

Câest lâhonneur de la France, et ce devrait Ãªtre lâhonneur de toute lâEurope, que de se substituer Ã  cette alliance insincÃ¨re et de garantir la souverainetÃ© et lâintÃ©gritÃ© territoriale de lâArmÃ©nie. Pour Paris, cela passe par le renforcement du partenariat de dÃ©fense avec lâArmÃ©nie, que ce soit en matiÃ¨re de formation au combat âÂ notamment en montagneÂ â ou de contrats dâarmement.

Dans les contreforts du Caucase, lÃ  aussi, la recherche de la paix nâexclut pas âÂ mais, au contraire, nÃ©cessiteÂ â le rapport de force pour mettre un terme Ã  certaines ambitions impÃ©riales.

Â Â MaÅgorzata Gosiewska (ECR). â Wojna o GÃ³rski Karabach spowodowaÅa ogromny kryzys humanitarny, szczegÃ³lnie dotykajÄcy dzieci. Trzy lata temu jako wicemarszaÅek Sejmu RP zaprosiÅam do Polski dzieci z regionu Karabachu, sieroty, pÃ³Åsieroty, ktÃ³rych rodziny straciÅy domy i Årodki do Å¼ycia w wyniku dziaÅaÅ wojennych. AzerbejdÅ¼an odzyskaÅ kontrolÄ nad tym regionem, a na ludnoÅÄ ormiaÅskÄ naÅoÅ¼ono blokadÄ prowadzÄcÄ do niedoborÃ³w Å¼ywnoÅci, lekarstw, przerw w dostawach prÄdu. W efekcie nastÄpiÅ exodus Ormian, w tym dzieci. Czym dla dziecka jest utrata bliskich, a potem domu rodzinnego? To utrata wszystkiego, co do tej pory znaÅy i kochaÅy. SpoÅecznoÅÄ miÄdzynarodowa ma obowiÄzek upominaÄ siÄ o przestrzeganie praw czÅowieka, zwÅaszcza praw dzieci i nieÅÄ im stale pomoc. O co apelujÄ.

Â Â Helmut BrandstÃ¤tter (Renew). â MrÂ President, MrÂ Commissioner, if I understood you rightly, you said that COP29 is a chance for MrÂ Aliyev to show that there are human rights in Azerbaijan. I'm afraid he doesn't care for human rights. He probably doesn't even know the word. If I think back to Sochi 2014, what happened a few months after Sochi? Did Putin care for human rights? No, he occupied Crimea.

And with Aliyev you also see a clear pattern. He cracks down on anyone who dares to criticise him. And this way we are even legitimising COP29. The billions of euros he spends on luxury properties in London, Dubai and wherever else is not his money. He stole it from the people of Azerbaijan.

What is also a topic: he does not only throw people into the prison in his country or kill people in his country, he even does it in Europe. There's the case of Vidadi Isgandarli. He was attacked in France by three men and died from his injuries. So we have to see Azerbaijan. It's a cruel dictatorship and it's wrong to give him the COP29.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Toussaint (Verts/ALE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, nous discutons aujourdâhui de lâAzerbaÃ¯djan, parce que Bakou sâapprÃªte Ã  accueillir une confÃ©rence mondiale pour le climat et quâÃ©videmment on demande Ã  celles et ceux qui dÃ©noncent ce fait-lÃ  de se taire. Eh bien, nous ne nous tairons pas, parce que la dÃ©fense du climat ne peut se faire au dÃ©triment des droits de lâhomme. Jamais.

La vÃ©ritÃ©, câest quâil aurait fallu boycotter cette COP et suspendre les accords gaziers tant que lâintÃ©gritÃ© territoriale de lâArmÃ©nie nâest pas pleinement et absolument respectÃ©e, quâun accord de paix nâest pas signÃ© et que les prisonniers politiques azerbaÃ¯djanais et armÃ©niens ne sont pas libÃ©rÃ©s.

Alors, disons ce qui doit Ãªtre dit: Aliyev muselle la presse pour cacher, entre autres, ses relations avec la Russie et avec lâindustrie fossile Ã©cocidaire, mais aussi pour masquer ses attaques rÃ©pÃ©tÃ©es contre ses opposants politiques, qui subissent parfois la torture. IlhamÂ Aliyev nâest pas un dÃ©mocrate.

Hier encore, pourtant, la Commission europÃ©enne signait de nouveaux contrats gaziers avec le rÃ©gime azerbaÃ¯djanais, indiffÃ©rente Ã  lâÃ©puration ethnique Ã  lâÅuvre dans le Haut-Karabakh. Câest une faute morale et politique majeure. Nous lâavons dÃ©noncÃ©e, car nous connaissons notre histoire et que nous nâavons pas oubliÃ© le gÃ©nocide subi par le peuple armÃ©nien. Mais ce souvenir ne suffit pas.

Alors, il faut aujourdâhui agir, pour faire reculer une fois pour toutes le rÃ©gime dâAliyev.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Pernando Barrena Arza (The Left). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±orÃ­as, hace un aÃ±o AzerbaiyÃ¡n culminÃ³ una limpieza Ã©tnica de absolutamente toda la poblaciÃ³n armenia de Nagorno Karabaj, su tierra ancestral, donde los armenios habÃ­an vivido durante miles de aÃ±os, una limpieza Ã©tnica precedida de todo tipo de crÃ­menes de guerra.

En este momento, la situaciÃ³n de lo que ocurre allÃ­ ha quedado desdibujada por la magnitud de otras crisis y conflictos, pero la realidad es que decenas de miles de armenios de Karabaj ahora estÃ¡n refugiados fuera de su hogar y hay decenas de presos polÃ­ticos en poder de BakÃº.

Las conversaciones entre Armenia y AzerbaiyÃ¡n pueden ser un buen paso para dar paz y estabilidad a la regiÃ³n, pero no podemos olvidar la realidad de los karabajÃ­es ni tomar AzerbaiyÃ¡n por un paÃ­s aliado de la UniÃ³n Europea por mucho gas que tenga o por mucha diplomacia del caviar que siga ejerciendo en los pasillos de esta Casa. No podemos olvidar que AzerbaiyÃ¡n es un Estado autoritario donde se detiene a periodistas, activistas y defensores de derechos humanos, se reprimen protestas pacÃ­ficas y se restringe ilegÃ­timamente el trabajo de medios de comunicaciÃ³n y organizaciones independientes.

Europa no puede mirar hacia otro lado porque necesite compensar el embargo a Rusia: el gas azerÃ­ que llega a Europa lo hace manchado de sangre armenia y de la vulneraciÃ³n de derechos humanos contra la ciudadanÃ­a de AzerbaiyÃ¡n.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Massimiliano Salini (PPE). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, nel settembre del 2023 le 100.000 persone che abitavano la regione del Nagorno-Karabakh sono state, di fatto, deportate attraverso una un'operazione di pulizia etnica, come giustamente Ã¨ stata definita.

L'Europa ha reagito ma timidamente. Ed Ã¨ inaccettabile che continuino le forniture â senza condizioni â di gas che arricchiscono un Paese totalmente sprezzante dello Stato di diritto, come l'Azerbaigian, Paese che ha addirittura definito l'operazione di cui parliamo un'operazione "contro il terrorismo armeno". Addirittura il territorio non viene definito neanche armeno: viene definito l'Â«Azerbaigian occidentaleÂ». Questo prelude a quello che potrebbe accadere in futuro.

Bene, la cosa piÃ¹ grave, perÃ², Ã¨ la violenza e il cinismo con cui tutti i segni della presenza cristiana-armena in Nagorno-Karabakh vengono distrutti. Quello dimostra la falsitÃ  e la mistificazione della narrazione azera e ci incarica di una grande responsabilitÃ , come cristiani europei, verso quel popolo.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â RaphaÃ«l Glucksmann (S&D). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, chers collÃ¨gues, vous pouvez donc enfermer les opposants et les journalistes, vous pouvez mettre en pÃ©ril la vie de personnes aussi courageuses que le DrÂ Ibadoghlou, vous pouvez poignarder vos opposants jusque dans les rues de nos villes, vous pouvez commettre un nettoyage ethnique dans le Haut-Karabakh et menacer constamment votre voisin, lâArmÃ©nie, vous pouvez contourner les sanctions europÃ©ennes contre le rÃ©gime poutinien en nous vendant le gaz russe, que nous boycottons par ailleursâ¦ et vous pouvez quand mÃªme recevoir la terre entiÃ¨re en grande pompe lors de la COP.

Câest profondÃ©ment lunaire. Cette tyrannie gaziÃ¨re organise la lutte internationale contre le rÃ©chauffement climatique. La faiblesse de nos dirigeants vis-Ã -vis de ce rÃ©gime est insigne.

Alors il est temps. Il est temps de dÃ©fendre les dissidents qui ont trouvÃ© refuge sur notre sol. Il est temps de soutenir plus fortement lâArmÃ©nie, de suspendre lâaccord gazier entre lâUnion europÃ©enne et lâAzerbaÃ¯djan, de montrer que les crimes ont des consÃ©quences, mÃªme quand vous jonglez avec les milliards. Il est temps de montrer que nous avons encore quelques principes et que nous ne sommes pas prÃªts Ã  les vendre.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marion MarÃ©chal (ECR). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, il y a un an, je rencontrais les rÃ©fugiÃ©s de lâArtsakh en ArmÃ©nie. Jâai encore en tÃªte leurs tÃ©moignages poignants: blocus et sous-nutrition, meurtres, bombardements de civils, profanations dâÃ©glises et de cimetiÃ¨res. Comment ne pas condamner cette Ã©puration ethnique? Mais cela nâest pas suffisant. Il est impÃ©ratif que les chancelleries europÃ©ennes appliquent des sanctions fortes contre cette dictature de lâAzerbaÃ¯djan, qui musÃ¨le des journalistes et retient des prisonniers politiques de lâArtsakh.

Nous condamnons lâhypocrisie de la PrÃ©sidente de la Commission, qui se fÃ©licitait alors de son accord Ã©nergÃ©tique avec ce quâelle qualifiait de Â«partenaire fiableÂ». Loin de nous soustraire Ã  la dÃ©pendance au gaz russe, nous continuons de lâimporter en Europe via lâAzerbaÃ¯djan. Nous devons boycotter la COPÂ 29, qui se dÃ©roulera Ã  Bakou, tant que ces rÃ©fugiÃ©s nâauront pas lâassurance de pouvoir revenir en sÃ©curitÃ© sur leurs terres et tant que la protection de leurs biens culturels ne sera pas assurÃ©e. La vie dâun rÃ©fugiÃ© de lâArtsakh ne vaut pas moins que le gaz azerbaÃ¯djanais.

Enfin, lâArmÃ©nie ne peut pas rester seule face aux vellÃ©itÃ©s expansionnistes de lâAzerbaÃ¯djan et de la Turquie. LâEurope doit la soutenir, y compris par des moyens militaires, pour lui permettre de garantir son intÃ©gritÃ© territoriale et de nÃ©gocier la paix Ã  son avantage. Câest un enjeu de civilisation pour nous, et il en va de notre honneur.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Silvia Sardone (PfE). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, da anni assistiamo alla tragedia degli armeni e dei cristiani: pulizia etnica, crimini di guerra, chiese distrutte. Nel Nagorno-Karabakh abbiamo visto una politica distruttiva supportata dalla Turchia, che ha portato alla fuga di migliaia di persone. Cosa volete fare per tutelare il patrimonio millenario armeno e cristiano?

L'Unione europea Ã¨ zitta e cieca. Silenzio anche sui prigionieri detenuti oramai da anni. Il popolo armeno deve sopportare il continuo tentativo della sua cancellazione come enclave cristiana da parte di nuove dittature, spesso supportate da milizie di fanatici jihadisti.

Nel mondo ci sono 365 milioni di cristiani perseguitati. Il Parlamento europeo perÃ² si preoccupa solo di islamofobia, quando l'Europa o Ã¨ cristiana o non Ã¨. Ed evidentemente per la maggioranza qui va bene anche non essere nulla.

A me tutto ciÃ² non va bene. Io non ci sto. Bisogna alzare la testa e difendere ciÃ² che siamo, le nostre radici cristiane.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Petras AuÅ¡treviÄius (Renew). â MrÂ President, Commissioner, dear colleagues, first of all, I would like to reassure our opponents for their allegedly unfounded criticism of Azerbaijan: the Council of Europe suspended the country's membership in the Parliamentary Assembly at the beginning of this year â a clear proof. This is the result of a long-term and systematic crackdown by the Baku authorities on political opponents, dissidents, non-governmental organisations and human rights activists.

As we speak, dear colleagues at plenary tonight, the health of Gubad Ibadoghlu is in a very, very critical state. But authorities do not allow him to travel abroad for proper medical treatment. Let me call on EU leaders, who willingly will take part in COP29 meeting, to bring out Gubad Ibadoghlu by their planes from Baku. It's the only way to save his life.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Catarina Vieira (Verts/ALE). â MrÂ President, dear Commissioner, FamilÂ Khalilov, AnarÂ Mamadli, BahruzÂ Samadov, IqbalÂ Abilov. These are among the ever-growing list of political prisoners in Azerbaijan â a list that also includes GubadÂ Ibadoghlu, shortlisted for this year's Sakharov Prize. Jailed under dubious charges and currently under house arrest, he is being detained and denied the urgent medical care that he requires.

Yet, Commission PresidentÂ vonÂ derÂ Leyen refers to Azerbaijan as a reliable partner, signaling perhaps that protecting oil and gas imports matters more than upholding our values. We must use the approaching COP29 in Baku to shine a spotlight on Azerbaijan's human rights situation and prevent the regime from misusing the events to whitewash its reputation.

Let us, in words and in action, reaffirm that a reliable partnership is one built not just on economic interests, but also on shared values.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Reinhold Lopatka (PPE). â MrÂ President, dear colleagues, our Lithuanian colleague mentioned that the Council of Europe opposed to give Azerbaijan credentials for the Parliamentary Assembly of the Council of Europe. I was one of these 75. We are now blacklisted by Azerbaijan. We are not allowed to enter this country, even on official missions. That is the real face of Azerbaijan.

There are currently six inter-state cases pending before the European Court of Human Rights concerning Azerbaijan. In addition, Freedom House has designated Azerbaijan as one of the most authoritarian states globally. Arbitrary arrests, torture are a common feature in the country, and more than 300 individuals are currently in prison for political charges.

At the heart of all this, now the COP conference is coming to Baku next month. I think we should not give Azerbaijan a chance to create a false impression of a country of tranquillity and of respect for human rights. This is not Azerbaijan: it is the opposite.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Alessandra Moretti (S&D). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, poche volte, anche in quest'Aula, abbiamo sentito parlare di Nagorno-Karabakh. Eppure il conflitto tra Azerbaigian e Armenia ha causato centinaia di vittime e il trasferimento forzato di una popolazione: un vero crimine contro l'umanitÃ . Le autoritÃ  azere continuano a ridurre gli spazi della societÃ  civile, chiudendo ONG e arrestando giornalisti con dubbie accuse.

Proprio mentre accade tutto questo, l'Azerbaigian, perÃ², ha ricevuto un grande appoggio per diventare paese ospite della COP29: Baku vuole presentarsi al mondo come un Paese dall'irreprensibile profilo istituzionale.

Ma non possiamo farci incantare dall'utilizzo diplomatico del gas azero svendendo i nostri principi per interessi economici. Ogni accordo tra UE e Azerbaigian deve essere condizionato al rispetto dei diritti umani e del diritto internazionale, fino anche a considerare la sospensione dell'attuale memorandum.

Non solo: chiediamo indagini sulle atroci violenze compiute nel Nagorno-Karabakh e che i responsabili siano sanzionati, per poi lavorare ad un processo di pace costruttivo e rispettoso tra Azerbaigian e Armenia.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

ÎÏÎ¸Îµ Î· ÏÏÎ± Î½Î± Î²Î¬Î»Î¿ÏÎ¼Îµ Î­Î½Î± ÏÎ­Î»Î¿Ï ÏÎµ ÏÏÎ¿ÏÏ Î´ÎµÎ½ ÏÎ­Î²Î¿Î½ÏÎ±Î¹ ÏÎ¿ Î´Î¹ÎµÎ¸Î½Î­Ï Î´Î¯ÎºÎ±Î¹Î¿ ÎºÎ±Î¹ Î½Î± ÏÎ­ÏÎ¿ÏÎ¼Îµ ÏÎ¿ ÎÎ¶ÎµÏÎ¼ÏÎ±ÏÏÎ¶Î¬Î½ ÏÏÎ¿ ÏÏÎ½ ÎµÏÎ¸ÏÎ½ÏÎ½ ÏÎ¿Ï.

Â Â Michael McNamara (Renew). â Mr President, colleagues, Azerbaijan is a country that I came to know well when I worked in the OSCE, came to know even better when I was a member of the Parliamentary Assembly of the Council of Europe, just beside this building, where I, along with a lot of others, encountered what was called at the time 'caviar diplomacy', where very large sums of money were washing around to encourage people to ignore Azerbaijan's human rights record.

Because of that, I suppose, I wasn't surprised to meet a delegation from Azerbaijan in the Irish parliament building as streams of refugees were leaving Nagorno-Karabakh. Money talks, and nothing talks as loudly as petrodollars. For similar reasons, I'm not surprised that COP29 is going to be held there, and a minister of the Irish government, no less, is going to go over there and expound his environmental credentials to the world.

Nevertheless, I think it's also time to be positive and to look at the possibilities that this offers. If there is a possibility of peace, then I would urge the AzeriÂ Government to seize that opportunity, because otherwise COP29 will be no more than a farce. I would urge them to seize a possibility of peace with Armenia.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lena Schilling (Verts/ALE). â MrÂ President, 'My father is suffering. He's in pain. His heart aneurism is getting worse. If his arteries rupture, he will die and I will lose my father forever. He's being held hostage by the Azerbaijani Government for investigating the corrupt oil and gas sector before the COP. I'm begging the world to save my father.' This message comes from Zhala, a daughter who's fighting for her father, DrÂ Gubad. And she's not alone: there are 300 innocent political prisoners currently imprisoned in Azerbaijan.

To the Azerbaijani Government, I say this: we will not allow you to greenwash your actions ahead of the COP. We will not let you bury the truth. We will shine a light on your violent crackdown on human rights and inhumanity. We demand the immediate release of political prisoners and to get Zhala's father the medical treatment he needs. Dignity and human rights are something we fight for everywhere.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Davor Ivo Stier (PPE). â PoÅ¡tovani predsjedavajuÄi, kolegice i kolege, promjene u meÄunarodnom poretku dovele su do toga da su se promijenili odnosi i na JuÅ¾nom Kavkazu i jasno je da je uÄvrÅ¡Äena pozicija AzerbajdÅ¾ana, ali to ne daje pravo AzerbajdÅ¾anu da krÅ¡i ljudska prava.

AzerbajdÅ¾an ima pravo na svoj teritorijalni integritet, ali ne na krÅ¡enje ljudskih prava. Mora poÅ¡tivati pravo Armenaca da se vrate u svoje domove u Gorskom Karabahu.

Europska unija ima razvijene odnose s AzerbajdÅ¾anom, ima energetsku suradnju, ali ima i politiÄki dijalog i to mora iskoristiti da naglasi kako se moraju poÅ¡tivati ljudska prava.

Moramo puno snaÅ¾nije podrÅ¾ati i naÅ¡e kolege iz Parlamentarne skupÅ¡tine VijeÄa Europe. I ja sam u tom broju koji je na crnoj listi AzerbajdÅ¾ana jer smo samo digli glas protiv krÅ¡enja ljudskih prava.

Na kraju, Å¾elio bih pozvati Baku da iskoristi ovu situaciju i da sklopi mir s Armenijom za stabilnost cijele regije JuÅ¾nog Kavkaza.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Costas Mavrides (S&D). â MrÂ President, Azerbaijan's aggression to force the displacement of a 100Â 000 indigenous Armenian population of Nagorno-Karabakh remains a crime against humanity, while today prisoners of war are still being held captive, and Armenia's territory is violated.

As for Azerbaijan's record for human rights and fundamental freedoms, it is simply deplorable, with a wave of arrests and criminal cases against human rights defenders and journalists.

We do welcome the Commissioner's strong words, but we also strongly denounce the President of the Commission's statement that characterised Azerbaijan as a 'trustworthy energy supplier'. The Aliyev regime does not even respect the International Court of Justice order to respect the religious and historical heritage.

And a final word: we also deplore any attempt to facilitate in any way the international recognition of the secessionist entity in the occupied part of the Republic of Cyprus, as promoted by the criminal regime of ErdoÄan and Aliyev at the same time.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Bert-Jan Ruissen (ECR). â Voorzitter, vorig jaar bezocht ik de Lachin-corridor, waar ik onder andere sprak met ArmeniÃ«rs uit Nagorno-Karabach. EÃ©n keer raden hoe de ambassadeur van Azerbeidzjan reageerde? Hij plaatste een afbeelding van een geweer op X, inclusief de dreigende boodschap dat ik me daar niet nog een keer moest laten zien. Dit ontoelaatbare incident valt natuurlijk in het niet bij wat de mensen uit Nagorno-Karabach moeten ervaren: verbanning uit hun regio, vernietiging van hun religieus erfgoed en blijvende militaire aspiraties van Aliyev richting ArmeniÃ«. En dan de bevolking van Azerbeidzjan zelf. Zij worden ernstig beperkt in hun rechten. Ik denk bijvoorbeeld aan Gubad Ibadoghlu.

Ondertussen blijven de Raad en de Commissie Azerbeidzjan beschouwen als een betrouwbare partner. Ik vind dat onbegrijpelijk. Het wordt de hoogste tijd voor een andere koers, Te beginnen met de instelling van sancties tegen personen die de mensenrechten schenden en de soevereiniteit van ArmeniÃ« bedreigen.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Emma Wiesner (Renew). â MrÂ President, we are approaching the global climate conference and our focus right now should be on the global existential climate crisis.

But at the same time, we cannot ignore what is happening all over Azerbaijan, in Baku and in the host country of the COP29. Azerbaijan has violating human rights for a really long time, but this year they have really been stepping it up. Opposition figures, journalists, fighters and civil rights defenders are thrown into jail, just like GubadÂ Ibadoghlu, one of the finalists for the Sakharov Prize this year, nominated for his work on anti-corruption in the oil and gas sector, kept in house arrest in a frightening medical condition.

If that wasn't enough, Azerbaijan is also engaged in a war with ethnic cleansing of Armenians in Nagorno-Karabakh, a clear violation of international law. And given these violations, it's a shame that the UN framework allows a country like Azerbaijan to host the COP29. Lets have now the world's attention on Azerbaijan to keep that on the government, to remind them of their international obligations and to make sure that regimes like Azerbaijan cannot host the COP in the future.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Michael Bloss (Verts/ALE). â Mr President, dear colleagues, what a shame! The climate conference will once again be held in a fossil autocracy. Azerbaijan profits from fossil fuels while repressing its own people. And it should not be entrusted with the presidency of the climate conference. I know â and we know â that Azerbaijan's security is watching us right now as we expose their dirty business, because President Aliyev is scared of the truth getting out.

DrÂ Gubad Ibadoghlu's case â that we all refer to here â makes it painfully clear: a respected economist who dared to speak out against Azerbaijan's corrupt regime. For this he was imprisoned, he was tortured, and now he suffers from heart failure. President Aliyev, give your citizens the freedom they deserve and release DrÂ Ibadoghlu immediately.

And to my colleagues: DrÂ Ibadoghlu is a nominee for the Sakharov Prize. Let's award him this prize and save his life.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â ÎÎ¿ÏÎºÎ¬Ï Î¦Î¿ÏÏÎ»Î¬Ï (PPE). â ÎÏÏÎ¹Îµ Î ÏÏÎµÎ´ÏÎµ, ÎºÏÏÎ¹Îµ ÎÏÎ¯ÏÏÎ¿ÏÎµ, ÏÏÎ¿ ÎÎ¶ÎµÏÎ¼ÏÎ±ÏÏÎ¶Î¬Î½, ÏÎ¿Î½ Î±Î¾Î¹ÏÏÎ¹ÏÏÎ¿ ÏÎ¬ÏÎ± ÎµÏÎ±Î¯ÏÎ¿, Î²Î±ÏÎ±Î½Î¯Î¶Î¿Î½ÏÎ±Î¹ ÎºÎ±Î¹ ÏÏÎ»Î±ÎºÎ¯Î¶Î¿Î½ÏÎ±Î¹ ÏÎ¿Î»Î¹ÏÎ¹ÎºÎ¿Î¯ ÏÎ¿Ï Î±Î½ÏÎ¹ÏÎ¯Î¸ÎµÎ½ÏÎ±Î¹ ÏÏÎ¿ ÎºÎ±Î¸ÎµÏÏÏÏ, Î´Î·Î¼Î¿ÏÎ¹Î¿Î³ÏÎ¬ÏÎ¿Î¹ ÏÎ¿Ï ÏÏÎ»Î¼Î·ÏÎ±Î½ Î½Î± ÏÎ¿ ÎµÏÎ¹ÎºÏÎ¯Î½Î¿ÏÎ½ ÏÏÎ»Î±ÎºÎ¯Î¶Î¿Î½ÏÎ±Î¹, ÎÏÎ¼Î­Î½Î¹Î¿Î¹ Î±Î¹ÏÎ¼Î¬Î»ÏÏÎ¿Î¹ ÏÎ¿Î»Î­Î¼Î¿Ï ÎºÎ±Î¸Î·Î¼ÎµÏÎ¹Î½Î¬ Î´Î­ÏÎ¿Î½ÏÎ±Î¹ ÏÎ¿Î½ ÎµÎ¾ÎµÏÏÎµÎ»Î¹ÏÎ¼Ï ÎºÎ±Î¹ ÏÎ·Î½ ÏÎ±ÏÎµÎ¯Î½ÏÏÎ· ÏÏÎ¿ ÎÎ±Î³ÎºÏÏÎ½Î¿ ÎÎ±ÏÎ±Î¼ÏÎ¬Ï, Î´ÎµÎºÎ¬Î´ÎµÏ ÏÎ¹Î»Î¹Î¬Î´ÎµÏ ÎÏÎ¼Î­Î½Î¹Î¿Î¹ ÎµÎºÎ´Î¹ÏÏÎ¸Î·ÎºÎ±Î½ Î±ÏÏ ÏÎ± ÏÏÎ¯ÏÎ¹Î± ÏÎ¿ÏÏ, ÎºÎ±Î¸ÎµÏÎ¯ ÏÏÎ¹ÏÏÎ¹Î±Î½Î¹ÎºÏ ÎºÎ±ÏÎ±ÏÏÏÎ­ÏÎµÏÎ±Î¹.
